message.content:
Research PatternFly design system for React to understand available components and best practices.

Use WebSearch and WebFetch to research:
1. PatternFly React components catalog (https://www.patternfly.org/)
2. PatternFly charts library (@patternfly/react-charts)
3. PatternFly table components for data grids
4. PatternFly dashboard patterns
5. PatternFly navigation patterns (sidebar, tabs)
6. PatternFly responsive design approach

Focus on finding:
- Components that match our UX specs (dashboards, data tables, charts)
- How to set up PatternFly with Vite + React + TypeScript
- Storybook integration with PatternFly
- Chart types available (area, line, bar, donut, etc.)
- Table features (sorting, filtering, pagination, selection)

Provide a mapping of our UX requirements to PatternFly components.

content.text: I'll research the PatternFly design system for React to understand available components and best practices for your project.

Based on my search, here's what I found about PatternFly dashboard patterns and responsive design:

## PatternFly Dashboard Patterns - Responsive Design

### Key Responsive Design Principles

Responsive design is primarily important when determining card priority in dashboards. When a four-column layout becomes a one-column layout on a mobile viewport, ordering the cards appropriately will make sure that the most important information comes to the top.

### Card Sizing and Layout

PatternFly recommends using three standard sizes for cards: small, medium, and large. This sizing helps prepare for determining column structure. These sizes refer to the overall surface area of cards, not specifically the length or width.(no content)You can use any common amount of columns, but three and four column layouts are the most common. For the purpose of the tutorial, they work with a standard four column layout with 16px gutters and 16px margins around the dashboard.

### Card Layouts

PatternFly supports several common card layouts to help convey information in a consistent way. While not an exhaustive list, card layouts are flexible and can be customized. Standard layouts are recommended as a basis for building custom designs.

### Combining Layouts for Responsiveness

The dashboard pattern combines the grid layout with flex to improve the responsiveness of information. Similarly, the primary-detail pattern combines the stack layout with flex to ensure icons and buttons are responsive to changes in screen size.

### Framework Support

For React and Core (HTML/CSS), PatternFly provides 8 types of layouts to visualize data in a responsive manner. For example, the Grid layout works almost the same way as Bootstrap columns.(no content)PatternFly uses a mobile-first approach (based on Bootstrap-3) for creating websites and applications which makes it more responsive.

### Dashboard Components

A dashbar is a smaller dashboard placed inside each application or dashboard to provide essential information for immediate action. It's always placed at the top and takes up the upper half of the screen.

For more detailed implementation examples, you can visit the official PatternFly documentation at [patternfly.org/patterns/dashboard/design-guidelines/](https://www.patternfly.org/patterns/dashboard/design-guidelines/).


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.

Based on my search, here's what I found about PatternFly's React charts library:

## @patternfly/react-charts

This library provides a set of React chart components for use with the PatternFly reference implementation. The latest version is 8.4.0, published about 3 months ago.

### Installation

You can install the library with `yarn add @patternfly/react-charts yarn add victory`. Note that Victory is now an optional peer dependency, allowing future chart libraries to be installed without including Victory dependencies and vice versa. You may choose to install the single "victory" package to cover all features, or install packages based on the features used in your app (e.g., "victory-core", "victory-tooltip", etc.).

### Foundation

PatternFly React charts are based on the Victory chart library, along with additional functionality, custom components, and theming for PatternFly. This provides a collection of React based components you can use to build PatternFly patterns.

### CSS Handling

This library makes use of the babel plugin from @patternfly/react-styles to enable providing the CSS alongside the components. This removes the need for consumers to use (style|css|sass)-loaders.(no content)Default styles in the @patternfly/react-charts package are aligned with the light theme. Charts work with PatternFly's light theme by defaultвҖ”you don't need to import anything else.

### Available Components

The library includes various chart components such as:
- Chart, ChartArea, ChartAxis, ChartBar
- ChartDonut, ChartDonutUtilization
- ChartGroup, ChartLegend, ChartStack
- ChartThreshold, ChartThemeColor, ChartTooltip
- ChartVoronoiContainer, ChartBullet, ChartPie

### Chart Types

Area charts are used to show (potentially multiple) trends over a continuous scale (usually time). Bar charts are used to show and compare categories and their respective data point/value, and can be plotted vertically or horizontally.(no content)Donut charts are used to show the relationship of a set of values to a whole or to show progress completion as a percentage. Donut charts are best used to display data with a small number of categories (example: 2-5 categories). Use the middle of the chart to highlight a data point.

For more information, visit:
- npm: https://www.npmjs.com/package/@patternfly/react-charts
- PatternFly documentation: https://www.patternfly.org/charts/about-charts/
- GitHub: https://github.com/patternfly/patternfly-react


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.

Here are the search results for PatternFly React components catalog 2026:

## PatternFly React Components

PatternFly provides a set of React components for the PatternFly project.(no content)PatternFly is Red Hat's open source design system, consisting of components, documentation, and code for building enterprise applications at scale.

### Main React Packages

The PatternFly React implementation is composed of several npm packages:
- **@patternfly/react-core**: Most React core components
- **@patternfly/react-charts**: PatternFly charting components
- **@patternfly/react-code-editor**: A PatternFly wrapper for the Monaco code editor(no content)The @patternfly/react-core library provides common React components for the PatternFly reference implementation, with the latest version being 6.4.0.

### Catalog View Extension

There is a separate react-catalog-view package that contains components based on PatternFly, with development previously done under the patternfly-react repo.

### Recent Updates (PatternFly 6)

Link catalog tiles in the catalog view extension have been updated to be actionable cards in PatternFly 6.(no content)PatternFly has added new components to support advanced AI/agentic interactions, and resizable table columns have been added within the @patternfly/react-data-view extension, allowing users to click and drag column dividers to adjust widths.

### PatternFly Elements

PatternFly Elements is a set of community-created web components based on PatternFly design. You can pick and choose from the list of components, and keep page payloads small as the components range from ~3 kB to ~10 kB in size minified and gzipped.

For full component documentation and the complete catalog, visit the official [PatternFly website](https://www.patternfly.org) or the [GitHub repository](https://github.com/patternfly/patternfly-react).


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.

Here are the search results for PatternFly Vite React TypeScript setup integration:

## PatternFly with Vite, React, and TypeScript

### Official PatternFly Setup Options

PatternFly provides an open-source build scaffolding utility to help developers jump start new projects. For TypeScript, you can fork and clone the PatternFly React seed project. For JavaScript, you can fork and clone the JavaScript branch of the PatternFly React seed project.(no content)PatternFly supports the 2 most recent versions of React at a time. PatternFly 6 currently supports React 17 and 18, with support for React 19 coming soon.

### PatternFly React Seed Project

Patternfly Seed is an open source build scaffolding utility for web apps. The primary purpose of this project is to give developers a jump start when creating new projects that will use PatternFly. A secondary purpose of this project is to serve as a reference for how to configure various aspects of an application that uses PatternFly, webpack, react, typescript, etc.(no content)Out of the box you'll get an app layout with chrome (header/sidebar), routing, build pipeline, test suite, and some code quality tools. Basically, all the essentials.

### PatternFly React Packages

PatternFly's React library contains a collection of components that you can use to build interfaces with consistent markup, styling, and behavior. It includes packages like @patternfly/react-core (most React core components), @patternfly/react-charts (charting components), @patternfly/react-code-editor (Monaco code editor wrapper), and @patternfly/react-icons (icons as React components).

### Using @patternfly/react-core

Each PatternFly component already contains the CSS related to the component. In order to have consistent styles throughout the application, PatternFly Base CSS should be used in the project, or some components may diverge in appearance. This import statement should be placed before your main application component is imported.

The basic import pattern is:
```typescript
import '@patternfly/react-core/dist/styles/base.css';
import { Button } from '@patternfly/react-core';
```

### Vite + PatternFly Issues

There have been reported issues with Vite and PatternFly. Vite works fine in dev mode. However, when running `npm run build` (vite build), there can be incorrect files generated and incorrect path references in the index.js. It's unclear if this is a @patternfly/react-core issue or a Vite issue.

### PatternFly Elements (Web Components Alternative)

For PatternFly Elements (web components), with the Vite setup complete, you need to install @patternfly/elements and the @lit/react library. You can then import the various components in your App.tsx file.(no content)PatternFly Elements ships with React wrappers for each web component. These wrappers make working with web components within react JSX files more convenient.

### Vite + React + TypeScript General Setup

Setting up a React project with TypeScript and Vite creates a powerful, efficient, and modern development environment that accelerates both your workflow and application performance. By leveraging Vite's lightning-fast development server and Rollup-powered production builds, developers can focus more on writing clean, scalable code without being bogged down by slow build times or complex configuration.(no content)Vite handles TypeScript seamlessly, transpiling .ts and .tsx files using esbuild under the hood (just for speed), while deferring type-checking to tsc or vue-tsc (if needed). This hybrid approach balances speed and correctness, keeping development snappy while still allowing for type safety.


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.

Here are the search results for PatternFly table data grid sorting filtering pagination:

## PatternFly Table & Data Grid Components

### Overview
PatternFly is Red Hat's open source design system. It consists of components, documentation, and code for building enterprise applications at scale.(no content)PatternFly offers 2 components for displaying large data sets: data lists and tables. While they satisfy similar use cases, choosing the correct component to use in your design will be dependent on the type of data you need to display.

### Toolbar Features
The toolbar sits above the table and contains controls for manipulating table data. Common actions include filtering, sorting, and pagination.(no content)Bulk selection, when present, selects all items in a table. If pagination is being used, this will only select items on the current page.

### Sorting
Table data can only be sorted on one column at a time.(no content)Sorting by columns is possible for any table variation. Enabling the component within a table eases the ability to scan and read through the content. This option is favored over adding sorting functionality to the toolbar.(no content)When a column is sortable, the sort icon will appear to the right of the column header in a light grey color. Sorting will not become active until the user selects the column header. This triggers the arrow to point upwards and the content to be sorted in ascending order.(no content)PatternFly supports both simple sort and multi-column sort.

### Pagination
Pagination footer, when present, provides navigation to additional pages.(no content)Table View supports pagination. See Pagination for more details.

### Filtering
Filtering is optional. Users can see results of simple filters here. Results include the item and results count, list of active filters (with ability to remove individual filters), and button to clear all filters.

### When to Use Tables vs Data Lists
Use data lists when the information you want to display is not easily structured into a tabular format, content displayed varies between rows and you want a more flexible layout, or you plan to embed rich content like a chart or an image into a row.(no content)Use tables when the information you want to display is easily structured into a tabular format and the clarity of your content would benefit from well defined columns and headings.

### Compact vs Default Spacing
A table may sometimes need to be compact to make more rows visible at a time. The more rows you can see, the less you need to use pagination. Compact spacing is recommended for data with a simple structure.

### PatternFly Elements (Web Components)
A table is used to display large data sets that can be easily laid out in a simple grid with column headers. This is an initial release, with a subset of upstream PatternFly's features. APIs can be expected to change.

### Additional Table Features
PatternFly React Data Table features include customizable table headers and cells, customizable table footer with pagination, column visibility (ability to hide columns), row selection and hover for rows, inline actions with kebab menu, sortable columns with callbacks for sorting, and server side and client side paging.

For more details, visit:
- [PatternFly Table Design Guidelines](https://www.patternfly.org/components/table/design-guidelines/)
- [PatternFly Data List](https://www.patternfly.org/components/data-list/design-guidelines/)
- [PatternFly Elements Table](https://patternflyelements.org/components/table/)

Here are the search results for PatternFly table data grid sorting filtering pagination:

## PatternFly Table & Data Grid Components

### Overview
PatternFly is Red Hat's open source design system. It consists of components, documentation, and code for building enterprise applications at scale.(no content)PatternFly offers 2 components for displaying large data sets: data lists and tables. While they satisfy similar use cases, choosing the correct component to use in your design will be dependent on the type of data you need to display.

### Toolbar Features
The toolbar sits above the table and contains controls for manipulating table data. Common actions include filtering, sorting, and pagination.(no content)Bulk selection, when present, selects all items in a table. If pagination is being used, this will only select items on the current page.

### Sorting
Table data can only be sorted on one column at a time.(no content)Sorting by columns is possible for any table variation. Enabling the component within a table eases the ability to scan and read through the content. This option is favored over adding sorting functionality to the toolbar.(no content)When a column is sortable, the sort icon will appear to the right of the column header in a light grey color. Sorting will not become active until the user selects the column header. This triggers the arrow to point upwards and the content to be sorted in ascending order.(no content)PatternFly supports both simple sort and multi-column sort.

### Pagination
Pagination footer, when present, provides navigation to additional pages.(no content)Table View supports pagination. See Pagination for more details.

### Filtering
Filtering is optional. Users can see results of simple filters here. Results include the item and results count, list of active filters (with ability to remove individual filters), and button to clear all filters.

### When to Use Tables vs Data Lists
Use data lists when the information you want to display is not easily structured into a tabular format, content displayed varies between rows and you want a more flexible layout, or you plan to embed rich content like a chart or an image into a row.(no content)Use tables when the information you want to display is easily structured into a tabular format and the clarity of your content would benefit from well defined columns and headings.

### Compact vs Default Spacing
A table may sometimes need to be compact to make more rows visible at a time. The more rows you can see, the less you need to use pagination. Compact spacing is recommended for data with a simple structure.

### PatternFly Elements (Web Components)
A table is used to display large data sets that can be easily laid out in a simple grid with column headers. This is an initial release, with a subset of upstream PatternFly's features. APIs can be expected to change.

### Additional Table Features
PatternFly React Data Table features include customizable table headers and cells, customizable table footer with pagination, column visibility (ability to hide columns), row selection and hover for rows, inline actions with kebab menu, sortable columns with callbacks for sorting, and server side and client side paging.

For more details, visit:
- [PatternFly Table Design Guidelines](https://www.patternfly.org/components/table/design-guidelines/)
- [PatternFly Data List](https://www.patternfly.org/components/data-list/design-guidelines/)
- [PatternFly Elements Table](https://patternflyelements.org/components/table/)

input.prompt: What are the core PatternFly components, installation steps, and best practices for React integration?
What are PatternFly dashboard patterns and best practices for layout and responsiveness?
What chart types are available in PatternFly and how to use them?

content.content:
# PatternFly Core Components, Installation, and React Best Practices

## Core Components

PatternFly's React library includes several npm packages:

- **@patternfly/react-core**: "Most React core components"
- **@patternfly/react-charts**: Charting functionality
- **@patternfly/react-icons**: Icon components
- **@patternfly/react-table**: Table components
- **@patternfly/react-tokens**: CSS variables as JavaScript objects

## Installation Steps for React

For new projects, the recommended approach is using the PatternFly React seed, which provides scaffolding with TypeScript or JavaScript variants.

For existing projects, install the core package:
```
npm install @patternfly/react-core --save
```
or with Yarn:
```
yarn add @patternfly/react-core
```

## React Integration Best Practices

1. **Version Support**: "PatternFly supports the 2 most recent versions of React at a time." Currently React 17 and 18 are supported.

2. **Configuration Support**: The system accommodates TypeScript, Webpack, Jest, and standard editor configurations.

3. **Package Compatibility**: All React packages release together, so check release highlights for compatible version combinations.

4. **Testing**: Components supporting Open UI Automation (OUIA) guidelines simplify automated testing implementation.

4. **Testing**: Components supporting Open UI Automation (OUIA) guidelines simplify automated testing implementation.

content.content:
# PatternFly Chart Types

PatternFly offers several chart types, each designed for specific data visualization needs:

**Core Chart Types:**

- **Area Chart**: "Use to show (potentially multiple) trends over a continuous scale (usually time)."

- **Bar Chart**: For displaying and comparing categories and their values, available in vertical or horizontal orientations.

- **Bullet Chart**: "Use as an extension to the bar chart to measure data with thresholds and static ranges."

- **Donut Chart**: "Use to show the relationship of a set of values to a whole or to show progress completion as a percentage."

- **Line Chart**: For comparing multiple datasets or showing data changes over time, particularly effective with small variations.

- **Pie Chart**: "Use to show percentages or proportional data."

- **Sparkline**: Small charts that visualize how values change over time without labeled axis values.

- **Stack Chart**: For displaying categorical data across multiple variables simultaneously.

**Additional Options:**

PatternFly also includes box plot, scatter, sankey, and threshold charts for specialized analytical needs.

Charts work best with legends when displaying multiple variables, and support interactive features like brush-and-zoom functionality and clickable legends to highlight or hide specific datasets.

content.content:
# PatternFly Dashboard Patterns and Best Practices

## Core Concept
"A dashboard provides overviews of key metrics or performance indicators relevant to an application, process, or business." Dashboards combine two essential elements: **cards** (displaying individual metrics) and **layout** (typically grid-based arrangements).

## Standard Card Types

PatternFly provides five common card patterns:

1. **Aggregate Status Cards** - Display total counts with status summaries (e.g., healthy vs. problematic items)
2. **Trend Cards** - Show metric changes over time using sparkline charts
3. **Utilization Cards** - Report current values using bar or donut charts
4. **Details Cards** - Present attribute-value pairs in structured formats
5. **Events Cards** - List time-based occurrences with filtering capabilities

## Design Process (6-Step Framework)

The documentation outlines a systematic approach:
- **Step 1:** List required cards based on use cases
- **Step 2:** Prioritize by importance across scenarios
- **Step 3:** Size cards (small, medium, large) based on content needs
- **Step 4:** Arrange on grid columns maintaining uniform widths and gutters
- **Step 5:** Add actual content and refine sizing
- **Step 6:** Iterate on final layout

## Responsiveness Considerations

When layouts shift from multi-column (desktop) to single-column (mobile), card ordering matters significantly. "The purpose of this step is to determine which cards want front and center, and which can receive less emphasis," particularly for responsive design.

## Layout Principles
- Use consistent column widths and gutters
- Avoid gaps created by dynamic cards expanding unevenly
- Consider content format when determining card dimensions (lists suit taller cards; charts suit wider ones)

Here are the search results for "PatternFly Storybook integration development environment":

## PatternFly and Storybook Integration

### PatternFly React (PatternFly 3)
The patternfly-react project uses React Storybook to test and demo new PatternFly 3 React UI components.(no content)This package provides Core PatternFly React components for PatternFly 3.

### PatternFly Elements
Storybook is an interactive tool that allows consumers of the components to see how content can flow inside the components, as well as how variants affect style and layout. The pfe-foo.storybook.js file within the /demo directory of a component defines what appears in the Storybook preview.

For development setup with PatternFly Elements:
- Storybook will launch at http://localhost:9001.
- Development and documentation servers load simultaneously on localhost:port `:8000` and `:8080` respectively.
- To get started, clone the repo and run: `git clone git@github.com:patternfly/patternfly-elements.git`, `cd patternfly-elements`, `npm ci` to install dependencies, then `npm run start`. Building PatternFly Elements requires specific versions of Node.js.

### Storybook Organization in PatternFly
Currently, the way patterns are organized in patternfly.org and the patternfly-react storybook do not align very well. As a first step to making the organization of components in storybook better aligned with the information architecture in pf.org, they introduced a third level of grouping to the storybook components.

### About Storybook
Storybook is an open-source tool for building, developing, and testing UI components in isolation. It provides a dedicated environment where designers and developers can create, preview, and document components without dealing with the complexities of integrating business logic or data. This isolation allows teams to focus on designing UIs, exploring edge cases, and ensuring consistency across projects.

### PatternFly Overview
PatternFly is Red Hat's open source design system. It consists of components, documentation, and code for building enterprise applications at scale.

Here are the search results for PatternFly sidebar navigation page layout application:

## PatternFly Page Component with Sidebar Navigation

The page component is used to define the basic layout of a page with either vertical or horizontal navigation.

### Key Components for Page Layout

A page will typically contain a `<Page>` with a masthead prop that often contains a masthead component. The `<MastheadMain>` component includes the smaller area that typically contains the `<MastheadToggle>` and a `<MastheadLogo>`. `<MastheadContent>` represents the main portion of the masthead, and will typically contain a `<Toolbar>` or other menu-like components. The `<MastheadToggle>` component contains a `<PageToggleButton>`, and the `<MastheadLogo>` component contains a `<MastheadBrand>`.

### Adding Sidebar Navigation

To add a vertical sidebar to a `<Page>`, pass a `<PageSidebar>` component into the sidebar property. To render navigation in the sidebar, pass a `<PageSidebarBody>` component to `<PageSidebar>`. The `isSidebarOpen` property helps facilitate the opening and closing of the sidebar and should be 'true' when the sidebar is visible.(no content)You can have multiple `<PageSidebarBody>` components inside the `<PageSidebar>` for more customization. You can modify the `<PageSidebarBody>` further by passing properties like `usePageInsets` (to match page insets), `isFilled={true}` (to grow to fill available vertical space), or `isFilled={false}` (to not grow to fill available space). By default, the last `<PageSidebarBody>` will grow to fill the available vertical space.

### Managed Sidebar

When the `isManagedSidebar` property is true, it manages the sidebar open/close state, removing the need to pass both `isSidebarOpen` into the `<PageSidebar>` and `onSidebarToggle` into the `<PageToggleButton>`.

### Page Sections

By default, page sections will not expand to fill in the available vertical space in a page. To change this default behavior, pass `isContentFilled` to the `<Page>` component and `isFilled={true}` to any child `<PageSection>` or `<PageGroup>` component that you want to fill the space. If multiple components are set to fill, then the available space will be split equally between them.

### Navigation Types

Vertical navigation is hierarchical global navigation that displays navigation options from top to bottom on the left side of a screen.(no content)To add horizontal navigation to the top of a `<Page>`, add the navigation inside of a `<ToolbarItem>` in the `<Toolbar>` that is passed to the `<MastheadContent>` of the `<Masthead>`.

### Sidebar Component (Separate from Page Sidebar)

A sidebar is a panel that splits content into a secondary area within a page. Sidebars orient a panel box and a content box on a page. The panel and content can be stacked on top of one another, or placed side-by-side. Sidebars can contain jumplinks and vertical tabs that provide easy access to different sections within a panel.


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.

     2вҶ’

   287вҶ’## 6. Gestalt Principles Application
   288вҶ’
   289вҶ’### 6.1 Proximity
   290вҶ’- **KPI Cards**: Grouped horizontally, equal spacing (24px gap)
   291вҶ’- **Fleet Panels**: Kinki Sharyo and Alstom adjacent, separated by 16px
   292вҶ’- **Related metrics**: Grouped within same card boundary
   293вҶ’
   294вҶ’### 6.2 Similarity
   295вҶ’- **Consistent card styling**: All panels use same border-radius (12px), shadow, padding
   296вҶ’- **Status colours**: Same palette across all components
   297вҶ’- **Typography**: Consistent font sizes per hierarchy level
   298вҶ’
   299вҶ’### 6.3 Enclosure
   300вҶ’- **Card boundaries**: Clear container borders for each widget
   301вҶ’- **Section headers**: Visual separation between dashboard sections
   302вҶ’- **Modal dialogs**: Distinct overlay with backdrop blur
   303вҶ’
   304вҶ’### 6.4 Continuity
   305вҶ’- **Timeline**: Continuous horizontal flow, left-to-right
   306вҶ’- **Reading pattern**: Z-pattern layout (top-left вҶ’ top-right вҶ’ bottom-left вҶ’ bottom-right)
   307вҶ’- **Data flow**: KPIs вҶ’ Status вҶ’ Actions вҶ’ Details
   308вҶ’
   309вҶ’### 6.5 Closure
   310вҶ’- **Progress bars**: User mentally completes the "fill" to understand percentage
   311вҶ’- **Trend indicators**: Arrow direction implies continuation of trend
   312вҶ’- **Abbreviated numbers**: "3.2K" understood as full number
   313вҶ’
   314вҶ’### 6.6 Figure-Ground
   315вҶ’- **Light background**: #F9FAFB (gray-50)
   316вҶ’- **Card background**: #FFFFFF with subtle shadow
   317вҶ’- **Focus elements**: Higher contrast, darker text
   318вҶ’

   354вҶ’
   355вҶ’### 8.1 Desktop (вүҘ1440px)

   378вҶ’## 9. Interaction Patterns
   379вҶ’
   380вҶ’### 9.1 KPI Card Interactions
   381вҶ’- **Hover**: Subtle elevation increase, show trend tooltip
   382вҶ’- **Click**: Navigate to detailed view (e.g., Fleet Availability вҶ’ Fleet Grid)
   383вҶ’- **Long press (mobile)**: Show quick actions menu
   384вҶ’
   385вҶ’### 9.2 Alert Interactions
   386вҶ’- **Dismiss**: Removes from view (can be restored from notification center)
   387вҶ’- **Action button**: Navigate to relevant section with context
   388вҶ’- **Expand**: Show full alert details inline
   389вҶ’
   390вҶ’### 9.3 Timeline Interactions
   391вҶ’- **Hover on marker**: Show PM details tooltip
   392вҶ’- **Click marker**: Open PM detail modal
   393вҶ’- **Drag**: Pan timeline left/right
   394вҶ’- **Pinch (touch)**: Zoom in/out
   395вҶ’
   396вҶ’### 9.4 Table Interactions
   397вҶ’- **Row click**: Navigate to train/assignment detail
   398вҶ’- **Column header click**: Sort ascending/descending
   399вҶ’- **Filter icon**: Open filter dropdown
   400вҶ’- **Checkbox**: Bulk selection for actions
   401вҶ’

   416вҶ’```

   418вҶ’### 10.3 Contextual Actions (Per Widget)
   419вҶ’
   420вҶ’- **Refresh**: Reload widget data
   421вҶ’- **Expand**: Full-screen view
   422вҶ’- **Export**: Download as PNG/CSV
   423вҶ’- **Configure**: Widget settings
   424вҶ’- **Help**: Contextual documentation
   425вҶ’
   426вҶ’---
   427вҶ’
   428вҶ’## 11. Data Refresh Strategy
   429вҶ’
   430вҶ’| Data Type | Refresh Interval | Source |
   431вҶ’|-----------|------------------|--------|
   432вҶ’| Fleet Status | Real-time (WebSocket) | Maximo Integration |
   433вҶ’| Mileage Data | Every 5 minutes | Daily Feed + Live Updates |
   434вҶ’| PM Schedule | Every 15 minutes | Scheduler Service |
   435вҶ’| Resource Data | Hourly | Resource Leveler Service |
   436вҶ’| Cost Data | Daily (overnight) | Cost Calculator Service |
   437вҶ’| Alerts | Real-time (Push) | Alert Service |
   438вҶ’
   439вҶ’**Visual Indicator:**
   440вҶ’- Last updated timestamp on each widget
   441вҶ’- Subtle pulse animation during refresh
   442вҶ’- Stale data warning (>30 min old)
   443вҶ’
   444вҶ’---
   445вҶ’
   446вҶ’## 12. Accessibility Requirements
   447вҶ’
   448вҶ’### 12.1 WCAG 2.1 AA Compliance
   449вҶ’- **Colour contrast**: Minimum 4.5:1 for text
   450вҶ’- **Focus indicators**: Visible focus ring on all interactive elements
   451вҶ’- **Keyboard navigation**: Full keyboard support with logical tab order
   452вҶ’- **Screen reader**: ARIA labels on all interactive elements
   453вҶ’
   454вҶ’### 12.2 Colour-Blind Considerations
   455вҶ’- Use icons + colour for status indication
   456вҶ’- Pattern fills in charts as alternative to colour only
   457вҶ’- High contrast mode option
   458вҶ’
   459вҶ’### 12.3 Motion Preferences
   460вҶ’- Respect `prefers-reduced-motion` media query
   461вҶ’- Disable auto-playing animations when set
   462вҶ’- Provide static alternatives to animated content
   463вҶ’
   464вҶ’---
   465вҶ’
   466вҶ’## 13. Unknown Unknowns Discovered
   467вҶ’
   468вҶ’### 13.1 Technical Unknowns
   469вҶ’1. **WebSocket reliability**: What happens when connection drops? Need reconnection strategy and offline indicators
   470вҶ’2. **Data staleness**: How to handle cases where Maximo feed is delayed >1 hour?
   471вҶ’3. **Cross-timezone operations**: Are all users in same timezone? How to display times for distributed teams?
   472вҶ’
   473вҶ’### 13.2 User Behaviour Unknowns
   474вҶ’4. **Widget customisation adoption**: Will users actually customise, or stick with defaults?
   475вҶ’5. **Alert fatigue**: How many alerts before users start ignoring them? Need smart grouping
   476вҶ’6. **Mobile usage patterns**: When/where do users access on mobile? Different feature set needed?
   477вҶ’
   478вҶ’### 13.3 Business Logic Unknowns
   479вҶ’7. **PM rescheduling authority**: Who can defer PM? What approval workflow needed?
   480вҶ’8. **Cost variance tolerance**: Is Вұ5% acceptable for all categories, or category-specific?
   481вҶ’9. **Run assignment conflicts**: What happens when Operations and Maintenance recommend different assignments?
   482вҶ’
   483вҶ’### 13.4 Integration Unknowns
   484вҶ’10. **Maximo sync latency**: What's typical delay between Maximo update and dashboard reflection?
   485вҶ’11. **Email notification reliability**: What if emails bounce? Need in-app fallback?
   486вҶ’12. **Report generation time**: Large reports (12-year projection) - sync or async?
   487вҶ’
   488вҶ’---
   489вҶ’
   490вҶ’## 14. Competitor Inspiration
   491вҶ’
   492вҶ’### 14.1 IBM Maximo
   493вҶ’- Role-based preset dashboards
   494вҶ’- KPI cards with drill-down
   495вҶ’- Work order list with status colours
   496вҶ’
   497вҶ’### 14.2 Siemens Rail
   498вҶ’- Geospatial map integration
   499вҶ’- Real-time performance indicators
   500вҶ’- Predictive analytics visualisation
   501вҶ’
   502вҶ’### 14.3 voestalpine zentrak
   503вҶ’- 360В° asset view
   504вҶ’- User-customisable KPI dashboard
   505вҶ’- Standard + custom KPI filters
   506вҶ’
   507вҶ’### 14.4 Modern SaaS Dashboards (Dribbble/Behance)
   508вҶ’- Dark mode support
   509вҶ’- Glassmorphism cards
   510вҶ’- Micro-interactions on hover
   511вҶ’- Data density with clarity
   512вҶ’
   513вҶ’---
   514вҶ’
   515вҶ’## 15. Implementation Notes
   516вҶ’
   517вҶ’### 15.1 Technology Recommendations
   518вҶ’- **Frontend**: React + TypeScript
   519вҶ’- **Charts**: Apache ECharts or Recharts
   520вҶ’- **State Management**: TanStack Query for server state
   521вҶ’- **Design System**: Tailwind CSS + Headless UI
   522вҶ’- **Real-time**: Socket.io for WebSocket
   523вҶ’
   524вҶ’### 15.2 Performance Targets
   525вҶ’- **First Contentful Paint**: < 1.5s
   526вҶ’- **Time to Interactive**: < 3s
   527вҶ’- **Largest Contentful Paint**: < 2.5s
   528вҶ’- **Cumulative Layout Shift**: < 0.1
   529вҶ’
   530вҶ’### 15.3 Testing Requirements
   531вҶ’- Visual regression testing with Playwright
   532вҶ’- Accessibility audit with axe-core
   533вҶ’- Performance monitoring with Lighthouse CI
   534вҶ’- E2E tests for all user flows
   535вҶ’
   536вҶ’---
   537вҶ’
   538вҶ’## 16. Sources & References
   539вҶ’
   540вҶ’### Fleet Management UX
   541вҶ’- [Hicron: Fleet Management Dashboard Design Guide](https://hicronsoftware.com/blog/fleet-management-dashboard-design/)
   542вҶ’- [Smashing Magazine: UX Strategies for Real-Time Dashboards](https://www.smashingmagazine.com/2025/09/ux-strategies-real-time-dashboards/)
   543вҶ’- [Aufait UX: Dashboard Design for Logistics](https://www.aufaitux.com/blog/dashboard-design-logistics-supply-chain-ux/)
   544вҶ’
   545вҶ’### Railway & Maintenance Systems
   546вҶ’- [ESRI: Railway Operations Management](https://www.esri.com/en-us/industries/rail/business-areas/operations)
   547вҶ’- [voestalpine: Asset & Maintenance Management](https://www.voestalpine.com/railway-systems/en/products/asset-and-maintenance-management/)
   548вҶ’- [Siemens: System Performance Dashboard](https://www.mobility.siemens.com/global/en/portfolio/rail-infrastructure/mass-transit/railway-mass-transit-analysis.html)
   549вҶ’
   550вҶ’### Design Principles
   551вҶ’- [Playfair Data: Applying Gestalt Principles to Dashboard Design](https://playfairdata.com/applying-gestalt-principles-to-dashboard-design/)
   552вҶ’- [BricxLabs: 15 Dashboard Design Principles for 2025](https://bricxlabs.com/blogs/tips-for-dashboard-design)
   553вҶ’- [Dev3lop: Gestalt Principles in Analytics Dashboard Design](https://dev3lop.com/gestalt-principles-applied-to-analytics-dashboard-design/)
   554вҶ’
   555вҶ’### Visual Inspiration
   556вҶ’- [Dribbble: Dark Mode Dashboard Designs](https://dribbble.com/tags/dark-mode-dashboard)
   557вҶ’- [Behance: Dashboard Analytics Projects](https://www.behance.net/search/projects/dashboard%20analytics)
   558вҶ’- [Muzli: 60+ Dashboard Design Inspiration](https://muz.li/inspiration/dashboard-inspiration/)
   559вҶ’
   560вҶ’### IBM Maximo
   561вҶ’- [IBM: Maximo Application Suite](https://www.ibm.com/products/maximo)
   562вҶ’- [IBM: Maximo for Transportation](https://www.ibm.com/docs/en/mft/7.6.2?topic=product-overview)
   563вҶ’
   564вҶ’---
   565вҶ’
   566вҶ’## 17. Appendix: Wireframe Grid System
   567вҶ’
   568вҶ’### 17.1 12-Column Grid

# Centralised Dashboard - UX Specification

**Document ID:** MILEAGE-UX-003
**Version:** 1.0
**Created:** 2026-01-08
**Status:** Design Specification
**RFQ Reference:** Section 5 - Software Output

---

## 1. Executive Summary

The Centralised Dashboard is the **primary entry point** for all users of the Mileage Management System. Per RFQ Section 5, it must provide:
- One **high-level customised output** (executive summary view)
- **Complete data and mileage status** (detailed operational view)

This document defines the UX architecture, wireframes, components, and interactions following Gestalt principles and industry best practices.

---

## 2. RFQ Requirements Mapping

| RFQ Requirement | Dashboard Component | Priority |
|-----------------|---------------------|----------|
| Centralised dashboard with high-level customised output | Hero KPI Cards + Executive Summary | P0 |
| Complete data, mileage status | Fleet Status Grid + Detailed Tables | P0 |
| Current maintenance status of all trains | Maintenance Status Panel | P0 |
| Train run assignments | Run Assignment Widget | P0 |
| Completed and upcoming PM/overhaul activities | PM Schedule Timeline | P0 |
| Maintenance compliance status | Compliance Gauge + Alerts | P0 |
| Monthly and yearly cost compliance | Cost Comparison Chart | P1 |
| Simulation report access | Quick Action Links | P1 |
| Email notification status | Notification Center | P2 |

---

## 3. User Roles & Views

### 3.1 Role-Based Dashboard Customisation

| Role | Primary Focus | Default Widgets |
|------|---------------|-----------------|
| **Fleet Manager** | Overall fleet health, compliance | KPI Cards, Fleet Grid, Compliance |
| **Maintenance Planner** | PM scheduling, resource allocation | PM Timeline, Resource Chart, Warnings |
| **Operations Manager** | Daily run assignments, fitness | Run Assignments, Fitness Status, Alerts |
| **Executive** | Cost compliance, high-level metrics | Executive Summary, Cost Charts, Trends |
| **Technician Supervisor** | Work orders, track allocation | Today's Work, Track Status, Backlog |

### 3.2 View Modes

1. **Executive View** (Default for Executives)
   - Single-page summary
   - Large KPI cards
   - Trend sparklines
   - No drill-down required

2. **Operational View** (Default for Planners/Managers)
   - Multi-panel layout
   - Interactive charts
   - Filterable tables
   - Drill-down capability

3. **Focus View** (Customisable)
   - User-selected widgets
   - Saved layouts per user
   - Quick switching

---

## 4. Information Architecture

### 4.1 Dashboard Hierarchy (Gestalt: Proximity + Enclosure)

### 4.2 Visual Hierarchy (Gestalt: Similarity + Continuity)

**Level 1 - Primary Focus (Top)**
- KPI Cards: Largest text, high contrast, immediate visibility
- Critical alerts: Red/amber visual weight

**Level 2 - Secondary Context (Middle)**
- Fleet status panels: Medium-weight cards
- PM timeline: Linear flow, left-to-right reading

**Level 3 - Supporting Detail (Bottom)**
- Charts: Data-dense but scannable
- Tables: Detailed information on demand

---

**KPI Card Configuration:**

| KPI | Formula | Warning Threshold | Critical Threshold |
|-----|---------|-------------------|-------------------|
| Fleet Availability | (FIT trains / Total) Г— 100 | < 90% | < 85% |
| PM Compliance | (On-time PM / Due PM) Г— 100 | < 95% | < 90% |
| Resource Utilisation | (Used man-hours / Available) Г— 100 | > 95% or < 70% | > 100% or < 60% |
| Cost Variance | ((Actual - Budget) / Budget) Г— 100 | > Вұ5% | > Вұ10% |

### 5.4 PM Schedule Timeline

**Timeline Configuration:**
- Default view: 30 days
- Zoom levels: 7d, 14d, 30d, 90d, 1Y
- Colour coding by PM level
- Hover reveals: Train ID, PM type, scheduled date, status

**PM Level Colours:**
| Level | Colour | Hex |
|-------|--------|-----|
| PM1 | Blue | #3B82F6 |
| PM2 | Indigo | #6366F1 |
| PM3 | Purple | #8B5CF6 |
| OVERHAUL | Orange | #F97316 |

**Status Markers:**
- в—Ҹ Filled circle: Completed
- в—Ӣ Empty circle: Scheduled
- в—җ Half-filled: In Progress
- вҠ— Crossed: Overdue

### 5.5 Resource Utilisation Chart

**Chart Type:** Stacked Area with Line Overlay (as designed in MILEAGE-UX-002)

**Data Series:**
1. **Available Man-hours** (Area, light blue fill)
2. **Adjusted Workload** (Area, green fill when under, red when over)
3. **Predicted Maintenance Workload** (Line, dashed orange)

**Gap Severity Colours:**
| Gap | Colour | Meaning |
|-----|--------|---------|
| > 500 | #22C55E Green | Comfortable capacity |
| 0 to 500 | #EAB308 Yellow | Near capacity |
| -500 to 0 | #F97316 Orange | Slight overallocation |
| < -500 | #EF4444 Red | Critical overallocation |

```

---

## 6. Gestalt Principles Application

### 6.1 Proximity
- **KPI Cards**: Grouped horizontally, equal spacing (24px gap)
- **Fleet Panels**: Kinki Sharyo and Alstom adjacent, separated by 16px
- **Related metrics**: Grouped within same card boundary

### 6.2 Similarity
- **Consistent card styling**: All panels use same border-radius (12px), shadow, padding
- **Status colours**: Same palette across all components
- **Typography**: Consistent font sizes per hierarchy level

### 6.3 Enclosure
- **Card boundaries**: Clear container borders for each widget
- **Section headers**: Visual separation between dashboard sections
- **Modal dialogs**: Distinct overlay with backdrop blur

### 6.4 Continuity
- **Timeline**: Continuous horizontal flow, left-to-right
- **Reading pattern**: Z-pattern layout (top-left вҶ’ top-right вҶ’ bottom-left вҶ’ bottom-right)
- **Data flow**: KPIs вҶ’ Status вҶ’ Actions вҶ’ Details

### 6.5 Closure
- **Progress bars**: User mentally completes the "fill" to understand percentage
- **Trend indicators**: Arrow direction implies continuation of trend
- **Abbreviated numbers**: "3.2K" understood as full number

### 6.6 Figure-Ground
- **Light background**: #F9FAFB (gray-50)
- **Card background**: #FFFFFF with subtle shadow
- **Focus elements**: Higher contrast, darker text

---

## 7. Colour System

### 7.1 Primary Palette

| Usage | Light Mode | Dark Mode |
|-------|------------|-----------|
| Background | #F9FAFB | #111827 |
| Card Background | #FFFFFF | #1F2937 |
| Primary Text | #111827 | #F9FAFB |
| Secondary Text | #6B7280 | #9CA3AF |
| Border | #E5E7EB | #374151 |
| Primary Action | #3B82F6 | #60A5FA |

### 7.2 Status Colours

| Status | Background | Text/Icon | Border |
|--------|------------|-----------|--------|
| Success | #F0FDF4 | #166534 | #22C55E |
| Warning | #FFFBEB | #854D0E | #F59E0B |
| Error | #FEF2F2 | #991B1B | #EF4444 |
| Info | #EFF6FF | #1E40AF | #3B82F6 |
| Neutral | #F9FAFB | #374151 | #D1D5DB |

### 7.3 Fleet Colours

| Fleet | Primary | Light | Dark |
|-------|---------|-------|------|
| Kinki Sharyo | #0891B2 | #CFFAFE | #164E63 |
| Alstom | #7C3AED | #EDE9FE | #4C1D95 |

---

## 8. Responsive Breakpoints

### 8.1 Desktop (вүҘ1440px)
- Full 4-column KPI layout
- Side-by-side panels
- All widgets visible

### 8.2 Laptop (1024px - 1439px)
- 4-column KPI (compressed)
- 2-column panel layout
- Horizontal scroll on tables

### 8.3 Tablet (768px - 1023px)
- 2-column KPI layout
- Stacked panels
- Collapsible sections

### 8.4 Mobile (< 768px)
- Single column
- Card-based layout
- Bottom navigation
- Swipe for more KPIs

---

## 9. Interaction Patterns

### 9.1 KPI Card Interactions
- **Hover**: Subtle elevation increase, show trend tooltip
- **Click**: Navigate to detailed view (e.g., Fleet Availability вҶ’ Fleet Grid)
- **Long press (mobile)**: Show quick actions menu

### 9.2 Alert Interactions
- **Dismiss**: Removes from view (can be restored from notification center)
- **Action button**: Navigate to relevant section with context
- **Expand**: Show full alert details inline

### 9.3 Timeline Interactions
- **Hover on marker**: Show PM details tooltip
- **Click marker**: Open PM detail modal
- **Drag**: Pan timeline left/right
- **Pinch (touch)**: Zoom in/out

### 9.4 Table Interactions
- **Row click**: Navigate to train/assignment detail
- **Column header click**: Sort ascending/descending
- **Filter icon**: Open filter dropdown
- **Checkbox**: Bulk selection for actions

---

## 10. Navigation Structure

### 10.1 Primary Navigation (Top Bar)

```
[Logo] | Dashboard | Planning | Assignments | Reports | Simulations | [Search] | [Notifications] | [User]
```

### 10.2 Dashboard Sub-navigation (Tabs)

```
[Overview] [Fleet Status] [Maintenance] [Resources] [Cost]
```

### 10.3 Contextual Actions (Per Widget)

- **Refresh**: Reload widget data
- **Expand**: Full-screen view
- **Export**: Download as PNG/CSV
- **Configure**: Widget settings
- **Help**: Contextual documentation

---

## 11. Data Refresh Strategy

| Data Type | Refresh Interval | Source |
|-----------|------------------|--------|
| Fleet Status | Real-time (WebSocket) | Maximo Integration |
| Mileage Data | Every 5 minutes | Daily Feed + Live Updates |
| PM Schedule | Every 15 minutes | Scheduler Service |
| Resource Data | Hourly | Resource Leveler Service |
| Cost Data | Daily (overnight) | Cost Calculator Service |
| Alerts | Real-time (Push) | Alert Service |

**Visual Indicator:**
- Last updated timestamp on each widget
- Subtle pulse animation during refresh
- Stale data warning (>30 min old)

---

## 12. Accessibility Requirements

### 12.1 WCAG 2.1 AA Compliance
- **Colour contrast**: Minimum 4.5:1 for text
- **Focus indicators**: Visible focus ring on all interactive elements
- **Keyboard navigation**: Full keyboard support with logical tab order
- **Screen reader**: ARIA labels on all interactive elements

### 12.2 Colour-Blind Considerations
- Use icons + colour for status indication
- Pattern fills in charts as alternative to colour only
- High contrast mode option

### 12.3 Motion Preferences
- Respect `prefers-reduced-motion` media query
- Disable auto-playing animations when set
- Provide static alternatives to animated content

---

## 13. Unknown Unknowns Discovered

### 13.1 Technical Unknowns
1. **WebSocket reliability**: What happens when connection drops? Need reconnection strategy and offline indicators
2. **Data staleness**: How to handle cases where Maximo feed is delayed >1 hour?
3. **Cross-timezone operations**: Are all users in same timezone? How to display times for distributed teams?

### 13.2 User Behaviour Unknowns
4. **Widget customisation adoption**: Will users actually customise, or stick with defaults?
5. **Alert fatigue**: How many alerts before users start ignoring them? Need smart grouping
6. **Mobile usage patterns**: When/where do users access on mobile? Different feature set needed?

### 13.3 Business Logic Unknowns
7. **PM rescheduling authority**: Who can defer PM? What approval workflow needed?
8. **Cost variance tolerance**: Is Вұ5% acceptable for all categories, or category-specific?
9. **Run assignment conflicts**: What happens when Operations and Maintenance recommend different assignments?

### 13.4 Integration Unknowns
10. **Maximo sync latency**: What's typical delay between Maximo update and dashboard reflection?
11. **Email notification reliability**: What if emails bounce? Need in-app fallback?
12. **Report generation time**: Large reports (12-year projection) - sync or async?

---

## 14. Competitor Inspiration

### 14.1 IBM Maximo
- Role-based preset dashboards
- KPI cards with drill-down
- Work order list with status colours

### 14.2 Siemens Rail
- Geospatial map integration
- Real-time performance indicators
- Predictive analytics visualisation

### 14.3 voestalpine zentrak
- 360В° asset view
- User-customisable KPI dashboard
- Standard + custom KPI filters

### 14.4 Modern SaaS Dashboards (Dribbble/Behance)
- Dark mode support
- Glassmorphism cards
- Micro-interactions on hover
- Data density with clarity

---

## 15. Implementation Notes

### 15.1 Technology Recommendations
- **Frontend**: React + TypeScript
- **Charts**: Apache ECharts or Recharts
- **State Management**: TanStack Query for server state
- **Design System**: Tailwind CSS + Headless UI
- **Real-time**: Socket.io for WebSocket

### 15.2 Performance Targets
- **First Contentful Paint**: < 1.5s
- **Time to Interactive**: < 3s
- **Largest Contentful Paint**: < 2.5s
- **Cumulative Layout Shift**: < 0.1

### 15.3 Testing Requirements
- Visual regression testing with Playwright
- Accessibility audit with axe-core
- Performance monitoring with Lighthouse CI
- E2E tests for all user flows

---

## 16. Sources & References

### Fleet Management UX
- [Hicron: Fleet Management Dashboard Design Guide](https://hicronsoftware.com/blog/fleet-management-dashboard-design/)
- [Smashing Magazine: UX Strategies for Real-Time Dashboards](https://www.smashingmagazine.com/2025/09/ux-strategies-real-time-dashboards/)
- [Aufait UX: Dashboard Design for Logistics](https://www.aufaitux.com/blog/dashboard-design-logistics-supply-chain-ux/)

### Railway & Maintenance Systems
- [ESRI: Railway Operations Management](https://www.esri.com/en-us/industries/rail/business-areas/operations)
- [voestalpine: Asset & Maintenance Management](https://www.voestalpine.com/railway-systems/en/products/asset-and-maintenance-management/)
- [Siemens: System Performance Dashboard](https://www.mobility.siemens.com/global/en/portfolio/rail-infrastructure/mass-transit/railway-mass-transit-analysis.html)

### Design Principles
- [Playfair Data: Applying Gestalt Principles to Dashboard Design](https://playfairdata.com/applying-gestalt-principles-to-dashboard-design/)
- [BricxLabs: 15 Dashboard Design Principles for 2025](https://bricxlabs.com/blogs/tips-for-dashboard-design)
- [Dev3lop: Gestalt Principles in Analytics Dashboard Design](https://dev3lop.com/gestalt-principles-applied-to-analytics-dashboard-design/)

### Visual Inspiration
- [Dribbble: Dark Mode Dashboard Designs](https://dribbble.com/tags/dark-mode-dashboard)
- [Behance: Dashboard Analytics Projects](https://www.behance.net/search/projects/dashboard%20analytics)
- [Muzli: 60+ Dashboard Design Inspiration](https://muz.li/inspiration/dashboard-inspiration/)

### IBM Maximo
- [IBM: Maximo Application Suite](https://www.ibm.com/products/maximo)
- [IBM: Maximo for Transportation](https://www.ibm.com/docs/en/mft/7.6.2?topic=product-overview)

---

## 17. Appendix: Wireframe Grid System

### 17.1 12-Column Grid

    10вҶ’
    11вҶ’## 1. Executive Summary
    12вҶ’
    13вҶ’The Train Mileage Status Grid is a **mission-critical component** displaying real-time status of all **128 trains** (49 Kinki Sharyo + 79 Alstom) in a data-dense, scannable format. This grid enables maintenance planners, fleet managers, and operations teams to quickly identify trains requiring attention, track PM windows, and monitor mileage balance across the fleet.
    14вҶ’
    15вҶ’**Key Design Decisions:**
    16вҶ’- **TanStack Table** for flexible, headless table logic
    17вҶ’- **Virtual scrolling** for performance with full fleet view
    18вҶ’- **25 rows per page** default with "Show All" option
    19вҶ’- **Sticky headers + first column** for context during scroll
    20вҶ’- **Status chips with colour coding** for instant visual scanning
    21вҶ’
    22вҶ’---
    23вҶ’
    24вҶ’## 2. User Requirements Analysis
    25вҶ’
    26вҶ’### 2.1 Primary User Tasks
    27вҶ’

    29вҶ’|------|-----------|----------|
    30вҶ’| Identify trains approaching PM | Daily | P0 |
    31вҶ’| Review fleet-wide mileage status | Daily | P0 |
    32вҶ’| Find specific train by ID | Multiple/day | P0 |
    33вҶ’| Filter by fleet type | Multiple/day | P1 |
    34вҶ’| Sort by mileage remaining | Daily | P1 |
    35вҶ’| Export data for reporting | Weekly | P2 |
    36вҶ’| Bulk select for PM scheduling | Weekly | P2 |
    37вҶ’
    38вҶ’### 2.2 Pain Points from Manual Excel Process (RFQ Background)
    39вҶ’- Difficult to scan 128 rows for urgent items
    40вҶ’- No real-time status updates
    41вҶ’- Manual sorting/filtering is tedious
    42вҶ’- Risk of missing overdue PM
    43вҶ’

   307вҶ’## 7. Performance Considerations
   308вҶ’
   309вҶ’### 7.1 Virtual Scrolling
   310вҶ’
   311вҶ’For "Show All" mode with 128 rows:
   312вҶ’- Use `react-window` or `@tanstack/virtual`
   313вҶ’- Render only visible rows + buffer (10 rows above/below)
   314вҶ’- Smooth 60fps scrolling
   315вҶ’- Maintain row height consistency (52px)
   316вҶ’
   317вҶ’### 7.2 Data Loading
   318вҶ’
   319вҶ’| Scenario | Strategy |
   320вҶ’|----------|----------|
   321вҶ’| Initial load | Load first page (25 rows) + metadata |
   322вҶ’| Pagination | Client-side (128 rows cacheable) |
   323вҶ’| Filtering | Client-side real-time |
   324вҶ’| Sorting | Client-side |
   325вҶ’| Full export | Server-side generation |
   326вҶ’
   327вҶ’### 7.3 Cache Strategy
   328вҶ’
   329вҶ’- Cache full fleet data for 5 minutes
   330вҶ’- WebSocket updates for status changes
   331вҶ’- Optimistic UI for user actions
   332вҶ’- Background refresh indicator
   333вҶ’
   334вҶ’---
   335вҶ’
   336вҶ’## 8. Accessibility (WCAG 2.1 AA)
   337вҶ’
   338вҶ’### 8.1 Keyboard Navigation
   339вҶ’
   340вҶ’| Key | Action |
   341вҶ’|-----|--------|
   342вҶ’| Tab | Move to next interactive element |
   343вҶ’| Arrow Keys | Navigate cells (when focus in grid) |
   344вҶ’| Space | Toggle checkbox / Expand row |
   345вҶ’| Enter | Activate row action / Confirm |
   346вҶ’| Escape | Close dropdown / Cancel |
   347вҶ’| Home/End | First/Last row |
   348вҶ’| Page Up/Down | Previous/Next page |
   349вҶ’
   350вҶ’### 8.2 Screen Reader Support
   351вҶ’
   352вҶ’- `role="grid"` on table container
   353вҶ’- `role="row"` and `role="gridcell"` on rows/cells
   354вҶ’- `aria-sort` on sortable headers
   355вҶ’- `aria-selected` on selected rows
   356вҶ’- `aria-label` on action buttons
   357вҶ’- Live region for filter result count
   358вҶ’
   359вҶ’### 8.3 Visual Accessibility
   360вҶ’
   361вҶ’- All status colours meet 4.5:1 contrast ratio
   362вҶ’- Icons + text (not colour alone) for status
   363вҶ’- Focus visible on all interactive elements
   364вҶ’- Reduced motion option for animations
   365вҶ’
   366вҶ’---

   368вҶ’## 9. Component Structure (React)
   369вҶ’
   370вҶ’```tsx
   371вҶ’<TrainMileageGrid>
   372вҶ’  <GridToolbar>
   373вҶ’    <FilterBar />
   374вҶ’    <SearchInput />
   375вҶ’    <ExportButton />
   376вҶ’  </GridToolbar>
   377вҶ’
   378вҶ’  <SummaryBar counts={fleetCounts} />
   379вҶ’
   380вҶ’  <DataGrid>
   381вҶ’    <GridHeader sticky>
   382вҶ’      <SelectAllCheckbox />
   383вҶ’      <SortableColumns />
   384вҶ’    </GridHeader>
   385вҶ’
   386вҶ’    <VirtualizedBody>
   387вҶ’      {trains.map(train => (
   388вҶ’        <GridRow key={train.id}>
   389вҶ’          <SelectCheckbox />
   390вҶ’          <TrainIdCell />
   391вҶ’          <FleetBadge />
   392вҶ’          <MileageCell />
   393вҶ’          <PMWindowProgress />
   394вҶ’          <StatusChip />
   395вҶ’          <NextPMCell />
   396вҶ’          <ActionsMenu />
   397вҶ’        </GridRow>
   398вҶ’      ))}
   399вҶ’    </VirtualizedBody>
   400вҶ’  </DataGrid>
   401вҶ’
   402вҶ’  <BulkActionsBar selected={selectedCount} />
   403вҶ’
   404вҶ’  <Pagination
   405вҶ’    total={128}
   406вҶ’    pageSize={25}
   407вҶ’    currentPage={1}
   408вҶ’  />
   409вҶ’</TrainMileageGrid>
   410вҶ’```

   470вҶ’## 11. Unknown Unknowns Discovered
   471вҶ’
   472вҶ’### 11.1 Data Integrity
   473вҶ’1. **Stale mileage data** - What if daily feed is delayed? Show last-updated timestamp
   474вҶ’2. **Conflicting sources** - Maximo vs. live feed discrepancy handling
   475вҶ’3. **Manual corrections** - How to handle user-corrected mileage entries?
   476вҶ’
   477вҶ’### 11.2 User Behaviour
   478вҶ’4. **Default view preference** - Should default sort persist per user?
   479вҶ’5. **Custom column order** - Allow users to reorder/hide columns?
   480вҶ’6. **Saved filter presets** - Enable "My overdue trains" quick filter?
   481вҶ’
   482вҶ’### 11.3 Edge Cases
   483вҶ’7. **New trains added** - How to highlight newly added fleet members?
   484вҶ’8. **Decommissioned trains** - Archive or hide? Historical data retention?
   485вҶ’9. **PM level transitions** - When train passes PM1, immediately show PM2?
   486вҶ’
   487вҶ’### 11.4 Performance
   488вҶ’10. **Concurrent users** - 50+ users viewing same grid, update synchronisation
   489вҶ’11. **Export large reports** - Background job for full 12-year projection export?
   490вҶ’12. **Mobile offline** - Cache critical data for field access?
   491вҶ’
   492вҶ’### 11.5 Business Logic
   493вҶ’13. **PM scheduling authority** - Who can schedule PM from grid?
   494вҶ’14. **Assignment override** - Can Operations override Maintenance recommendation?
   495вҶ’15. **Compliance audit trail** - Log all status changes for audit?
   496вҶ’

# Train Mileage Status Grid - UX Specification

**Document ID:** MILEAGE-UX-004
**Version:** 1.0
**Created:** 2026-01-08
**Status:** Design Specification
**RFQ Reference:** Section 5 - Current maintenance status of all trains in terms of mileage

---

## 1. Executive Summary

The Train Mileage Status Grid is a **mission-critical component** displaying real-time status of all **128 trains** (49 Kinki Sharyo + 79 Alstom) in a data-dense, scannable format. This grid enables maintenance planners, fleet managers, and operations teams to quickly identify trains requiring attention, track PM windows, and monitor mileage balance across the fleet.

**Key Design Decisions:**
- **TanStack Table** for flexible, headless table logic
- **Virtual scrolling** for performance with full fleet view
- **25 rows per page** default with "Show All" option
- **Sticky headers + first column** for context during scroll
- **Status chips with colour coding** for instant visual scanning

---

## 2. User Requirements Analysis

### 2.1 Primary User Tasks

| Task | Frequency | Priority |
|------|-----------|----------|
| Identify trains approaching PM | Daily | P0 |
| Review fleet-wide mileage status | Daily | P0 |
| Find specific train by ID | Multiple/day | P0 |
| Filter by fleet type | Multiple/day | P1 |
| Sort by mileage remaining | Daily | P1 |
| Export data for reporting | Weekly | P2 |
| Bulk select for PM scheduling | Weekly | P2 |

### 2.2 Pain Points from Manual Excel Process (RFQ Background)
- Difficult to scan 128 rows for urgent items
- No real-time status updates
- Manual sorting/filtering is tedious
- Risk of missing overdue PM

---

## 3. Grid Architecture

### 3.1 Layout Overview (Gestalt: Enclosure + Continuity)

```

```

### 3.2 Column Specification

| Column | Width | Sortable | Filterable | Description |
|--------|-------|----------|------------|-------------|
| Checkbox | 40px | No | No | Bulk selection |
| Train ID | 80px | Yes | Yes | Unique identifier (KS-001, AL-001) |
| Name | 120px | Yes | Yes | Human-readable name |
| Fleet | 100px | Yes | Yes (dropdown) | Kinki Sharyo / Alstom |
| Current Mileage | 110px | Yes | Yes (range) | Current km since last overhaul |
| PM Window | 150px | Yes | Yes | Visual progress bar + percentage |
| Fitness Status | 100px | Yes | Yes (dropdown) | FIT / RESTRICTED / UNFIT |
| Train Status | 110px | Yes | Yes (dropdown) | IN_SERVICE / IN_MAINTENANCE / OOS |
| Next PM | 100px | Yes | Yes (range) | PM level + days remaining |
| Last Service | 100px | Yes | Yes (date range) | Date of last PM |
| Today's Assignment | 120px | No | Yes | Route ID or "Unassigned" |
| Actions | 60px | No | No | Context menu |

**Total minimum width:** ~1190px (horizontal scroll on smaller screens)

---

## 4. Visual Design System

### 4.1 Status Colour Coding (Gestalt: Similarity)

**Fitness Status:**
| Status | Chip Colour | Background | Text | Icon |
|--------|-------------|------------|------|------|
| FIT | Green | #DCFCE7 | #166534 | вң“ |
| RESTRICTED | Amber | #FEF3C7 | #92400E | вҡ  |
| UNFIT | Red | #FEE2E2 | #991B1B | вң• |

**Train Status:**
| Status | Chip Colour | Background | Text |
|--------|-------------|------------|------|
| IN_SERVICE | Blue | #DBEAFE | #1E40AF |
| IN_MAINTENANCE | Purple | #EDE9FE | #5B21B6 |
| OUT_OF_SERVICE | Gray | #F3F4F6 | #374151 |

**PM Window Status (based on percentage to threshold):**
| Range | Background | Progress Bar | Text |
|-------|------------|--------------|------|
| 0-80% | #F0FDF4 | #22C55E (Green) | Safe |
| 80-90% | #FFFBEB | #F59E0B (Amber) | Approaching |
| 90-100% | #FEF2F2 | #EF4444 (Red) | Critical |
| >100% | #7F1D1D | Pulsing Red | OVERDUE |

### 4.2 PM Window Cell Design

```
в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
в”Ӯ  PM1 @ 50,000 km                           в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ   в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ  в”Ӯв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–‘в–‘в–‘в–‘в–‘в–‘в–‘в–‘в–‘в–‘в–‘в–‘в–‘в–‘в–‘в–‘в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ вҶҗ Progress bar (48,520/50,000)
в”Ӯ  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ   в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ  97% в”Ӯ 1,480 km remaining                  в”Ӯ
в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
```

### 4.3 Row Styling

**Alternating Rows (Zebra Stripes):**
- Even rows: #FFFFFF
- Odd rows: #F9FAFB

**Row States:**
- Hover: #F3F4F6 background
- Selected: #DBEAFE background, blue left border
- Focus: 2px blue outline
- Critical (PM overdue): #FEE2E2 background, pulsing subtle

**Row Height:** 52px (optimal for touch + dense data)

---

## 5. Interaction Patterns

### 5.1 Sorting

**Single-Column Sort:**
```
[Train ID вҶ‘] - Click header to sort
[Train ID вҶ“] - Click again to reverse
[Train ID]   - Click third time to clear
```

**Multi-Column Sort (Advanced):**
- Shift+Click to add secondary sort
- Display sort order indicators (1, 2, 3...)
- Clear all button in header

**Default Sort:** PM Window (Critical вҶ’ Approaching вҶ’ Safe)

### 5.2 Filtering

**Filter Bar Components:**

```
в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
в”Ӯ Fleet: [All в–ј]  Status: [All в–ј]  PM Due: [All в–ј]  Search: [__________рҹ”Қ]  в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ                                                                            в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ Active Filters: [Kinki Sharyo Г—] [PM Due: This Week Г—]     [Clear All]    в”Ӯ
в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
```

**Fleet Filter:**
- All Fleets
- Kinki Sharyo (49)
- Alstom (79)

**Status Filter:**
- All Statuses
- FIT (121)
- RESTRICTED (5)
- UNFIT (2)

**PM Due Filter:**
- All
- Overdue (critical)
- This Week
- This Month
- Next 3 Months

**Search:**
- Real-time filtering as user types
- Searches: Train ID, Name
- Minimum 2 characters
- Debounced 300ms

**Options:**
- 25 rows (default)
- 50 rows
- 100 rows
- Show All (with virtual scrolling)

- All columns visible

- Sticky first two columns

- Vertical filter stack

## 7. Performance Considerations

### 7.1 Virtual Scrolling

For "Show All" mode with 128 rows:
- Use `react-window` or `@tanstack/virtual`
- Render only visible rows + buffer (10 rows above/below)
- Smooth 60fps scrolling
- Maintain row height consistency (52px)

### 7.2 Data Loading

| Scenario | Strategy |
|----------|----------|
| Initial load | Load first page (25 rows) + metadata |
| Pagination | Client-side (128 rows cacheable) |
| Filtering | Client-side real-time |
| Sorting | Client-side |
| Full export | Server-side generation |

### 7.3 Cache Strategy

- Cache full fleet data for 5 minutes
- WebSocket updates for status changes
- Optimistic UI for user actions
- Background refresh indicator

---

## 8. Accessibility (WCAG 2.1 AA)

### 8.1 Keyboard Navigation

| Key | Action |
|-----|--------|
| Tab | Move to next interactive element |
| Arrow Keys | Navigate cells (when focus in grid) |
| Space | Toggle checkbox / Expand row |
| Enter | Activate row action / Confirm |
| Escape | Close dropdown / Cancel |
| Home/End | First/Last row |
| Page Up/Down | Previous/Next page |

### 8.2 Screen Reader Support

- `role="grid"` on table container
- `role="row"` and `role="gridcell"` on rows/cells
- `aria-sort` on sortable headers
- `aria-selected` on selected rows
- `aria-label` on action buttons
- Live region for filter result count

### 8.3 Visual Accessibility

- All status colours meet 4.5:1 contrast ratio
- Icons + text (not colour alone) for status
- Focus visible on all interactive elements
- Reduced motion option for animations

## 9. Component Structure (React)

```tsx
<TrainMileageGrid>
  <GridToolbar>
    <FilterBar />
    <SearchInput />
    <ExportButton />
  </GridToolbar>

  <SummaryBar counts={fleetCounts} />

  <DataGrid>
    <GridHeader sticky>
      <SelectAllCheckbox />
      <SortableColumns />
    </GridHeader>

    <VirtualizedBody>
      {trains.map(train => (
        <GridRow key={train.id}>
          <SelectCheckbox />
          <TrainIdCell />
          <FleetBadge />
          <MileageCell />
          <PMWindowProgress />
          <StatusChip />
          <NextPMCell />
          <ActionsMenu />
        </GridRow>
      ))}
    </VirtualizedBody>
  </DataGrid>

  <BulkActionsBar selected={selectedCount} />

  <Pagination
    total={128}
    pageSize={25}
    currentPage={1}
  />
</TrainMileageGrid>
```

```

---

## 11. Unknown Unknowns Discovered

### 11.1 Data Integrity
1. **Stale mileage data** - What if daily feed is delayed? Show last-updated timestamp
2. **Conflicting sources** - Maximo vs. live feed discrepancy handling
3. **Manual corrections** - How to handle user-corrected mileage entries?

### 11.2 User Behaviour
4. **Default view preference** - Should default sort persist per user?
5. **Custom column order** - Allow users to reorder/hide columns?
6. **Saved filter presets** - Enable "My overdue trains" quick filter?

### 11.3 Edge Cases
7. **New trains added** - How to highlight newly added fleet members?
8. **Decommissioned trains** - Archive or hide? Historical data retention?
9. **PM level transitions** - When train passes PM1, immediately show PM2?

### 11.4 Performance
10. **Concurrent users** - 50+ users viewing same grid, update synchronisation
11. **Export large reports** - Background job for full 12-year projection export?
12. **Mobile offline** - Cache critical data for field access?

### 11.5 Business Logic
13. **PM scheduling authority** - Who can schedule PM from grid?
14. **Assignment override** - Can Operations override Maintenance recommendation?
15. **Compliance audit trail** - Log all status changes for audit?

---

## 12. Technology Recommendations

### 12.1 Core Dependencies

```json
{
  "@tanstack/react-table": "^8.x",
  "@tanstack/react-virtual": "^3.x",
  "date-fns": "^3.x",
  "clsx": "^2.x"
}
```

### 12.2 Performance Budget

| Metric | Target |
|--------|--------|
| Initial render (25 rows) | < 100ms |
| Sort operation | < 50ms |
| Filter operation | < 50ms |
| Virtual scroll FPS | 60fps |
| Bundle size (grid component) | < 50KB |

---

## 13. Sources & References

### Data Table UX
- [Pencil & Paper: Enterprise Data Tables UX Patterns](https://www.pencilandpaper.io/articles/ux-pattern-analysis-enterprise-data-tables)
- [UX World: Data Table Design Best Practices](https://uxdworld.com/data-table-design-best-practices/)
- [Justinmind: Designing Effective Data Table UI](https://www.justinmind.com/ui-design/data-table)
- [UX Planet: Best Practices for Data Tables](https://uxplanet.org/best-practices-for-usable-and-efficient-data-table-in-applications-4a1d1fb29550)
- [Stephanie Walter: Essential Resources for Complex Data Tables](https://stephaniewalter.design/blog/essential-resources-design-complex-data-tables/)

### Technical Implementation
- [Strapi: Table in React Performance Guide](https://strapi.io/blog/table-in-react-performance-guide)
- [TanStack Table Documentation](https://tanstack.com/table/latest)
- [Simple Table: TanStack vs AG Grid Comparison](https://www.simple-table.com/blog/tanstack-table-vs-ag-grid-comparison)

### Accessibility
- [W3C: Grid Pattern (WAI-ARIA)](https://www.w3.org/WAI/ARIA/apg/patterns/grid/)
- [Deque: Accessible Data Tables](https://www.deque.com/blog/accessible-data-tables/)

---

1вҶ’# Man-Hour Projection Chart - UX Design Specification
     2вҶ’
     3вҶ’**Task ID:** MILEAGE-UX-002
     4вҶ’**Status:** CRITICAL - RFQ Section 8 Case Study
     5вҶ’**Created:** 2026-01-08
     6вҶ’
     7вҶ’---
     8вҶ’
     9вҶ’## 1. Executive Summary
    10вҶ’
    11вҶ’The Man-Hour Projection Chart is the **core visualization** for resource planning in the Rolling Stock Mileage Management System. It displays workload (man-hours required) vs capacity (man-hours available) over a 12-year contract period, enabling planners to:
    12вҶ’
    13вҶ’1. Identify resource overallocation/underallocation
    14вҶ’2. Compare "Without adjustment" vs "With adjustment" scenarios
    15вҶ’3. Make data-driven decisions about maintenance scheduling
    16вҶ’
    17вҶ’**RFQ Reference:** Section 8 - Case Studies (Page 4)
    18вҶ’
    19вҶ’---
    20вҶ’
    21вҶ’## 2. Chart Type Selection
    22вҶ’
    23вҶ’### Primary Chart: Stacked Area/Line Combination Chart
    24вҶ’
    25вҶ’Based on RFQ Section 8 and [industry best practices](https://www.projectmanager.com/blog/what-is-a-resource-histogram):
    26вҶ’
    27вҶ’| Chart Element | Type | Purpose |
    28вҶ’|---------------|------|---------|
    29вҶ’| Availability (Capacity) | Solid Area | Shows available man-hours as baseline |
    30вҶ’| Adjusted Workload | Line with markers | Shows scheduled/leveled work |
    31вҶ’| Predicted Maintenance Workload | Dashed line | Shows forecasted demand before adjustment |
    32вҶ’
    33вҶ’### Why Not a Pure Histogram?
    34вҶ’- RFQ shows **continuous time series** (28+ months)
    35вҶ’- Need to show **three overlapping data series**
    36вҶ’- Gap analysis requires **line comparison** not stacked bars
    37вҶ’
    38вҶ’---
    39вҶ’
    40вҶ’## 3. Visual Specification
    41вҶ’
    42вҶ’### 3.1 Dimensions
    43вҶ’
    44вҶ’```
    45вҶ’в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ

    48вҶ’в”Ӯ                                                                 в”Ӯ

   183вҶ’## 7. Accessibility
   184вҶ’
   185вҶ’### WCAG 2.1 AA Compliance
   186вҶ’
   187вҶ’| Requirement | Implementation |
   188вҶ’|-------------|----------------|
   189вҶ’| Color contrast | All text вүҘ 4.5:1 ratio |
   190вҶ’| Non-color indicators | Patterns + icons for status |
   191вҶ’| Keyboard navigation | Tab through data points |
   192вҶ’| Screen reader | ARIA labels for all elements |
   193вҶ’| Focus indicators | Visible focus ring on interactive elements |
   194вҶ’

   196вҶ’

   203вҶ’## 8. Data Model (TypeScript)
   204вҶ’
   205вҶ’```typescript
   206вҶ’// Chart data point for a single time period
   207вҶ’interface ManHourDataPoint {
   208вҶ’  period: {
   209вҶ’    year: number;
   210вҶ’    month: number;       // 1-12
   211вҶ’    label: string;       // "Jan-26"
   212вҶ’  };
   213вҶ’  taskCount: number;
   214вҶ’  predictedWorkload: number;    // Before adjustment
   215вҶ’  adjustedWorkload: number;     // After leveling
   216вҶ’  availableManHours: number;    // Capacity
   217вҶ’  availableManpower: number;    // Headcount
   218вҶ’  gap: number;                  // available - adjusted
   219вҶ’  utilizationPercent: number;   // (adjusted / available) * 100
   220вҶ’}
   221вҶ’
   222вҶ’// Chart configuration
   223вҶ’interface ManHourChartConfig {
   224вҶ’  timeRange: {
   225вҶ’    start: { year: number; month: number };
   226вҶ’    end: { year: number; month: number };
   227вҶ’  };
   228вҶ’  comparisonMode: 'without_adjustment' | 'with_adjustment' | 'side_by_side';
   229вҶ’  fleetFilter: 'ALL' | 'KINKI_SHARYO' | 'ALSTOM';
   230вҶ’  showDataTable: boolean;
   231вҶ’  showGapHighlighting: boolean;
   232вҶ’}
   233вҶ’
   234вҶ’// Chart state for UI
   235вҶ’interface ManHourChartState {
   236вҶ’  data: ManHourDataPoint[];
   237вҶ’  config: ManHourChartConfig;
   238вҶ’  hoveredPoint: ManHourDataPoint | null;
   239вҶ’  selectedPoint: ManHourDataPoint | null;
   240вҶ’  isLoading: boolean;
   241вҶ’  error: string | null;
   242вҶ’}
   243вҶ’
   244вҶ’// Gap severity classification
   245вҶ’type GapSeverity = 'comfortable' | 'near_capacity' | 'slight_over' | 'critical_over';
   246вҶ’
   247вҶ’function classifyGap(gap: number): GapSeverity {
   248вҶ’  if (gap > 500) return 'comfortable';
   249вҶ’  if (gap > 0) return 'near_capacity';
   250вҶ’  if (gap >= -500) return 'slight_over';
   251вҶ’  return 'critical_over';
   252вҶ’}
   253вҶ’```
   254вҶ’

   276вҶ’## 10. Integration Points
   277вҶ’
   278вҶ’### Data Sources
   279вҶ’- `ResourceLevelerService.getProjection(timeRange)` - Get man-hour projections
   280вҶ’- `SimulationService.compareScenarios()` - Compare adjustment strategies
   281вҶ’
   282вҶ’### Events Emitted
   283вҶ’- `onPeriodSelect(period)` - When user selects a specific month
   284вҶ’- `onAdjustmentRequest(period)` - When user wants to adjust a month
   285вҶ’- `onExport(format)` - When user exports chart/data
   286вҶ’
   287вҶ’### Events Consumed
   288вҶ’- `maintenanceScheduleUpdated` - Refresh chart data
   289вҶ’- `resourceConfigChanged` - Recalculate availability

   293вҶ’## 11. Performance Considerations
   294вҶ’
   295вҶ’| Scenario | Data Points | Strategy |
   296вҶ’|----------|-------------|----------|
   297вҶ’| 1 Year | 12 | Render all |
   298вҶ’| 5 Years | 60 | Render all |
   299вҶ’| 12 Years | 144 | Virtualize table, aggregate chart |
   300вҶ’
   301вҶ’### Optimization Strategies
   302вҶ’1. **Lazy load data table rows** beyond viewport
   303вҶ’2. **Aggregate to quarters** for views > 3 years
   304вҶ’3. **Debounce hover events** (100ms)
   305вҶ’4. **Memoize gap calculations**
   306вҶ’
   307вҶ’---
   308вҶ’
   309вҶ’## 12. Unknown Unknowns Discovered
   310вҶ’
   311вҶ’Through competitor research, several risks identified:
   312вҶ’
   313вҶ’| Risk | Impact | Mitigation |
   314вҶ’|------|--------|------------|
   315вҶ’| **Timezone issues** | Man-hours span midnight | Store in UTC, display in local |
   316вҶ’| **Holiday calendars** | Availability varies by country | Configurable working days |
   317вҶ’| **Overtime calculation** | Gap doesn't account for OT | Add "max overtime" parameter |
   318вҶ’| **Skill mismatch** | Man-hours available вү  qualified | Add skill-based filtering |
   319вҶ’| **Multi-depot planning** | Each depot has different capacity | Depot-level breakdown view |
   320вҶ’| **Emergency maintenance** | Unplanned work disrupts schedule | Reserve buffer capacity |
   321вҶ’

# Man-Hour Projection Chart - UX Design Specification

**Task ID:** MILEAGE-UX-002
**Status:** CRITICAL - RFQ Section 8 Case Study
**Created:** 2026-01-08

---

## 1. Executive Summary

The Man-Hour Projection Chart is the **core visualization** for resource planning in the Rolling Stock Mileage Management System. It displays workload (man-hours required) vs capacity (man-hours available) over a 12-year contract period, enabling planners to:

1. Identify resource overallocation/underallocation
2. Compare "Without adjustment" vs "With adjustment" scenarios
3. Make data-driven decisions about maintenance scheduling

**RFQ Reference:** Section 8 - Case Studies (Page 4)

---

## 2. Chart Type Selection

### Primary Chart: Stacked Area/Line Combination Chart

Based on RFQ Section 8 and [industry best practices](https://www.projectmanager.com/blog/what-is-a-resource-histogram):

| Chart Element | Type | Purpose |
|---------------|------|---------|
| Availability (Capacity) | Solid Area | Shows available man-hours as baseline |
| Adjusted Workload | Line with markers | Shows scheduled/leveled work |
| Predicted Maintenance Workload | Dashed line | Shows forecasted demand before adjustment |

### Why Not a Pure Histogram?
- RFQ shows **continuous time series** (28+ months)
- Need to show **three overlapping data series**
- Gap analysis requires **line comparison** not stacked bars

---

## 3. Visual Specification

### 3.1 Dimensions

```
