```

### 3.2 Color Palette

| Element | Color | Hex | Usage |
|---------|-------|-----|-------|
| Availability Area | Blue (muted) | `#3B82F6` opacity 30% | Capacity baseline |
| Capacity Line | Blue (solid) | `#3B82F6` | Capacity threshold |
| Adjusted Workload | Orange | `#F97316` | Scheduled work |
| Predicted Workload | Gray dashed | `#6B7280` | Unleveled forecast |
| Overallocation Zone | Red | `#EF4444` opacity 20% | Gap > 0 (demand > capacity) |
| Underallocation Zone | Green | `#22C55E` opacity 20% | Gap < 0 (capacity > demand) |

### 3.3 Data Labels

- Show values on line markers when hovered
- Show month labels on X-axis (abbreviated: Jan, Feb, Mar...)
- Show year change markers (vertical dashed line)
- Y-axis: Man-hours in increments of 1000 or auto-scaled

---

## 4. Data Table Component (Below Chart)

Per RFQ Section 8, a data table **must** accompany the chart:

в”ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”јв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”јв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”јв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”јв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”јв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”јв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Ө

в”Ӯ Gap(MH)  в”Ӯ   8   в”Ӯ  165   в”Ӯ -639   в”Ӯ  1031  в”Ӯ  230   в”Ӯ ...    в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ          в”Ӯ  рҹҹў   в”Ӯ  рҹҹў    в”Ӯ  рҹ”ҙ    в”Ӯ  рҹҹў    в”Ӯ  рҹҹў    в”Ӯ        в”Ӯ
в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ

### Gap Row Highlighting Rules

| Gap Value | Color | Icon | Meaning |
|-----------|-------|------|---------|
| Gap > 500 | Light Green | рҹҹў | Comfortable capacity |
| 0 < Gap вүӨ 500 | Yellow | рҹҹЎ | Near capacity |
| -500 вүӨ Gap < 0 | Orange | рҹҹ  | Slight overallocation |
| Gap < -500 | Red | рҹ”ҙ | Critical overallocation |

---

## 5. Interaction Design

### 5.1 Timeline Controls

```
[вҶҗ] [Jan 2026 в–ј] to [Dec 2027 в–ј] [вҶ’]  [1M] [3M] [6M] [1Y] [ALL]
```

- **Date pickers**: Start/end month selection
- **Quick ranges**: 1 month, 3 months, 6 months, 1 year, All (12 years)
- **Arrow navigation**: Shift view by selected range

### 5.2 Hover Interactions

When hovering over a data point:
```
в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
в”Ӯ February 2026          в”Ӯ
в”ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Ө
в”Ӯ Availability: 3,556 MH в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ Adjusted: 3,247 MH     в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ Predicted: 4,102 MH    в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ Gap: +309 MH           в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ Utilization: 91.3%     в”Ӯ
в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
```

### 5.3 Click Actions

- **Click on data point**: Opens detail panel for that month
- **Click on Gap cell**: Opens adjustment wizard
- **Double-click**: Zoom to selected month Вұ 3 months

### 5.4 Comparison Mode Toggle

```
[Without Adjustment] [With Adjustment] [Side-by-Side]
```

- **Without Adjustment**: Shows predicted workload without leveling
- **With Adjustment**: Shows leveled workload (optimized)
- **Side-by-Side**: Shows both charts stacked vertically

---

## 6. Responsive Design

### Desktop (вүҘ1200px)
- Full chart with data table below
- All columns visible in table
- Side panel for filters

### Tablet (768px - 1199px)
- Chart takes full width
- Data table scrolls horizontally
- Filters in collapsible header

### Mobile (< 768px)
- Simplified chart (key metrics only)
- Data table replaced with card-based view
- Swipe to navigate months

---

## 7. Accessibility

### WCAG 2.1 AA Compliance

| Requirement | Implementation |
|-------------|----------------|
| Color contrast | All text вүҘ 4.5:1 ratio |
| Non-color indicators | Patterns + icons for status |
| Keyboard navigation | Tab through data points |
| Screen reader | ARIA labels for all elements |
| Focus indicators | Visible focus ring on interactive elements |

### Alternative Views

- **Table-only mode**: For screen reader users
- **High contrast mode**: Black/white with patterns
- **Data export**: CSV/Excel for external analysis

---

## 8. Data Model (TypeScript)

```typescript
// Chart data point for a single time period
interface ManHourDataPoint {
  period: {
    year: number;
    month: number;       // 1-12
    label: string;       // "Jan-26"
  };
  taskCount: number;
  predictedWorkload: number;    // Before adjustment
  adjustedWorkload: number;     // After leveling
  availableManHours: number;    // Capacity
  availableManpower: number;    // Headcount
  gap: number;                  // available - adjusted
  utilizationPercent: number;   // (adjusted / available) * 100
}

// Chart configuration
interface ManHourChartConfig {
  timeRange: {
    start: { year: number; month: number };
    end: { year: number; month: number };
  };
  comparisonMode: 'without_adjustment' | 'with_adjustment' | 'side_by_side';
  fleetFilter: 'ALL' | 'KINKI_SHARYO' | 'ALSTOM';
  showDataTable: boolean;
  showGapHighlighting: boolean;
}

// Chart state for UI
interface ManHourChartState {
  data: ManHourDataPoint[];
  config: ManHourChartConfig;
  hoveredPoint: ManHourDataPoint | null;
  selectedPoint: ManHourDataPoint | null;
  isLoading: boolean;
  error: string | null;
}

// Gap severity classification
type GapSeverity = 'comfortable' | 'near_capacity' | 'slight_over' | 'critical_over';

function classifyGap(gap: number): GapSeverity {
  if (gap > 500) return 'comfortable';
  if (gap > 0) return 'near_capacity';
  if (gap >= -500) return 'slight_over';
  return 'critical_over';
}
```

---

## 9. Component Architecture

```
ManHourProjectionWidget/
в”ңв”Җв”Җ ManHourChart.tsx           # Main chart (recharts/visx)
в”ңв”Җв”Җ ManHourDataTable.tsx       # Data table below chart
в”ңв”Җв”Җ TimelineControls.tsx       # Date range selector
в”ңв”Җв”Җ ComparisonToggle.tsx       # Without/With adjustment toggle
в”ңв”Җв”Җ ChartTooltip.tsx           # Hover tooltip
в”ңв”Җв”Җ GapIndicator.tsx           # Gap cell with color coding
в”ңв”Җв”Җ ManHourDetailPanel.tsx     # Side panel for month details
в””в”Җв”Җ hooks/
    в”ңв”Җв”Җ useManHourData.ts      # Data fetching hook
    в”ңв”Җв”Җ useChartConfig.ts      # Configuration state
    в””в”Җв”Җ useGapAnalysis.ts      # Gap calculations
```

---

## 10. Integration Points

### Data Sources
- `ResourceLevelerService.getProjection(timeRange)` - Get man-hour projections
- `SimulationService.compareScenarios()` - Compare adjustment strategies

### Events Emitted
- `onPeriodSelect(period)` - When user selects a specific month
- `onAdjustmentRequest(period)` - When user wants to adjust a month
- `onExport(format)` - When user exports chart/data

### Events Consumed
- `maintenanceScheduleUpdated` - Refresh chart data
- `resourceConfigChanged` - Recalculate availability

---

## 11. Performance Considerations

| Scenario | Data Points | Strategy |
|----------|-------------|----------|
| 1 Year | 12 | Render all |
| 5 Years | 60 | Render all |
| 12 Years | 144 | Virtualize table, aggregate chart |

### Optimization Strategies
1. **Lazy load data table rows** beyond viewport
2. **Aggregate to quarters** for views > 3 years
3. **Debounce hover events** (100ms)
4. **Memoize gap calculations**

---

## 12. Unknown Unknowns Discovered

Through competitor research, several risks identified:

| Risk | Impact | Mitigation |
|------|--------|------------|
| **Timezone issues** | Man-hours span midnight | Store in UTC, display in local |
| **Holiday calendars** | Availability varies by country | Configurable working days |
| **Overtime calculation** | Gap doesn't account for OT | Add "max overtime" parameter |
| **Skill mismatch** | Man-hours available вү  qualified | Add skill-based filtering |
| **Multi-depot planning** | Each depot has different capacity | Depot-level breakdown view |
| **Emergency maintenance** | Unplanned work disrupts schedule | Reserve buffer capacity |

---

## 13. Sources & References

- [Resource Histogram Best Practices - ProjectManager.com](https://www.projectmanager.com/blog/what-is-a-resource-histogram)
- [Capacity Planning Software - The Digital Project Manager](https://thedigitalprojectmanager.com/tools/best-capacity-planning-software/)
- [Choosing Chart Types - Nielsen Norman Group](https://www.nngroup.com/articles/choosing-chart-types/)
- [Data Visualization Handbook - UX Magazine](https://uxmag.com/articles/the-ultimate-data-visualization-handbook-for-designers)
- [Stacked Bar Charts - Domo](https://www.domo.com/learn/charts/stacked-bar-charts)
- [Workload Management Tools - Runn](https://www.runn.io/blog/workload-management-tools)

---

## 14. Approval Checklist

- [ ] Reviewed by UX Designer
- [ ] Reviewed by Product Owner
- [ ] RFQ Section 8 compliance verified
- [ ] Accessibility audit passed
- [ ] Performance benchmarks met
- [ ] Approved for implementation

   554вҶ’## 8. Gestalt Principles Applied
   555вҶ’
   556вҶ’### 8.1 Proximity
   557вҶ’
   558вҶ’- **KPI Cards:** Grouped in a single row for quick scanning
   559вҶ’- **Chart + Legend:** Legend positioned close to chart
   560вҶ’- **Table + Actions:** Filter controls adjacent to table
   561вҶ’
   562вҶ’### 8.2 Similarity
   563вҶ’
   564вҶ’- **Status Colors:** Consistent across all views
   565вҶ’  - рҹҹў Green = Under budget / On target / Compliant
   566вҶ’  - рҹҹЎ Yellow = Warning / Approaching threshold
   567вҶ’  - рҹ”ҙ Red = Over budget / Overdue / Critical
   568вҶ’- **Card Design:** Same border radius, shadow, padding
   569вҶ’
   570вҶ’### 8.3 Enclosure
   571вҶ’
   572вҶ’- **Sections:** Clear card boundaries with subtle shadows
   573вҶ’- **Tables:** Header row visually distinct from data rows
   574вҶ’- **Charts:** Contained within labeled containers
   575вҶ’
   576вҶ’### 8.4 Continuity
   577вҶ’
   578вҶ’- **Trend Lines:** Smooth curves leading eye through time
   579вҶ’- **Waterfall:** Connected bars showing flow from budget to actual
   580вҶ’- **Progress Bars:** Horizontal continuity for completion status
   581вҶ’
   582вҶ’### 8.5 Figure-Ground
   583вҶ’
   584вҶ’- **Charts:** White background with colored data
   585вҶ’- **Alerts:** Elevated cards on lighter background
   586вҶ’- **Modal Dialogs:** Dimmed backdrop focuses attention
   587вҶ’
   588вҶ’### 8.6 Common Fate
   589вҶ’
   590вҶ’- **Interactive Highlights:** Hovering chart point highlights related table row
   591вҶ’- **Filter Application:** All components update simultaneously
   592вҶ’

   724вҶ’## 13. Unknown Unknowns Discovered
   725вҶ’
   726вҶ’### 13.1 Business Logic
   727вҶ’
   728вҶ’1. **Compliance aggregation:** How to weight sub-metrics into overall score?
   729вҶ’2. **Tolerance bands:** Different tolerances for different PM levels?
   730вҶ’3. **Cost allocation:** How are shared costs split between fleets?
   731вҶ’4. **Baseline adjustments:** When can budget baselines be updated?
   732вҶ’5. **Seasonality:** Are there seasonal patterns in mileage/cost compliance?
   733вҶ’
   734вҶ’### 13.2 UX Challenges
   735вҶ’
   736вҶ’6. **Alert fatigue:** How many alerts before users ignore them?
   737вҶ’7. **Drill-down depth:** How many levels before users get lost?
   738вҶ’8. **Comparison periods:** What comparisons are most valuable (MoM, YoY)?
   739вҶ’9. **Goal setting:** Who sets compliance targets and can they be adjusted?
   740вҶ’10. **Notification preferences:** Email vs in-app vs SMS for alerts?
   741вҶ’
   742вҶ’### 13.3 Technical Challenges
   743вҶ’
   744вҶ’11. **Historical data retention:** How far back to keep detailed data?
   745вҶ’12. **Data reconciliation:** Handling discrepancies between systems?
   746вҶ’13. **Offline access:** Do users need offline compliance reports?
   747вҶ’14. **Multi-currency:** Are there cross-border cost considerations?
   748вҶ’15. **Audit trail:** What compliance changes need to be logged?
   749вҶ’
   750вҶ’### 13.4 Integration
   751вҶ’
   752вҶ’16. **Maximo cost sync:** How are actual costs imported?
   753вҶ’17. **Budget import:** Source system for budget data?
   754вҶ’18. **External reporting:** Format for regulatory compliance reports?
   755вҶ’19. **Dashboard embedding:** Can dashboards be embedded elsewhere?
   756вҶ’20. **Single Sign-On:** Integration with enterprise identity?
   757вҶ’

# Maintenance Compliance Dashboard Specification

**Document ID:** MILEAGE-UX-006
**Version:** 1.0
**Created:** 2026-01-08
**RFQ Reference:** Sections 5, 6, 8

---

## 1. Overview

### 1.1 Purpose

This specification defines the user experience for the **Maintenance Compliance Dashboard**, enabling management to monitor PM schedule adherence, cost compliance against budget, and identify variance trends across the 128-train fleet over the 12-year contract period.

### 1.2 Target Users

| Role | Primary Tasks | View Focus |
|------|---------------|------------|
| Maintenance Manager | Monitor PM compliance, identify gaps | Schedule Adherence |
| Finance Controller | Track budget vs actual costs | Cost Compliance |
| Contract Manager | Ensure SLA adherence | KPI Summary |
| Executive | High-level oversight | Executive Summary |

### 1.3 RFQ Requirements Addressed

- **Section 5:** "Maintenance compliance status"
- **Section 5:** "Monthly/yearly cost compliance"
- **Section 6:** "Actual vs planned mileage" monitoring
- **Section 8:** Compliance reporting requirements

---

## 2. Information Architecture

### 2.1 Screen Hierarchy

```
Compliance Dashboard
в”ңв”Җв”Җ Executive Summary (Default)
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ KPI Cards
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ Compliance Score Gauge
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ Monthly Trend
в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җ Alert Summary
в”ңв”Җв”Җ Schedule Compliance
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ PM Compliance Rate
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ Backlog Status
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ Fleet Coverage
в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җ Overdue Analysis
в”ңв”Җв”Җ Cost Compliance
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ Budget vs Actual
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ Variance Analysis
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ Category Breakdown
в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җ Forecast Projection
в”ңв”Җв”Җ Mileage Compliance
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ Planned vs Actual
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ Fleet Balance
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ Deviation Trends
в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җ Train-Level Analysis
в””в”Җв”Җ Compliance Reports
    в”ңв”Җв”Җ Monthly Report
    в”ңв”Җв”Җ Annual Report
    в””в”Җв”Җ Custom Report Builder
```

### 2.2 Navigation Pattern

- **Tabs:** Executive | Schedule | Cost | Mileage | Reports
- **Breadcrumb:** Compliance > [Section] > [Period]
- **Period Selector:** Global control affecting all views

---

---

## 8. Gestalt Principles Applied

### 8.1 Proximity

- **KPI Cards:** Grouped in a single row for quick scanning
- **Chart + Legend:** Legend positioned close to chart
- **Table + Actions:** Filter controls adjacent to table

### 8.2 Similarity

- **Status Colors:** Consistent across all views
  - рҹҹў Green = Under budget / On target / Compliant
  - рҹҹЎ Yellow = Warning / Approaching threshold
  - рҹ”ҙ Red = Over budget / Overdue / Critical
- **Card Design:** Same border radius, shadow, padding

### 8.3 Enclosure

- **Sections:** Clear card boundaries with subtle shadows
- **Tables:** Header row visually distinct from data rows
- **Charts:** Contained within labeled containers

### 8.4 Continuity

- **Trend Lines:** Smooth curves leading eye through time
- **Waterfall:** Connected bars showing flow from budget to actual
- **Progress Bars:** Horizontal continuity for completion status

### 8.5 Figure-Ground

- **Charts:** White background with colored data
- **Alerts:** Elevated cards on lighter background
- **Modal Dialogs:** Dimmed backdrop focuses attention

### 8.6 Common Fate

- **Interactive Highlights:** Hovering chart point highlights related table row
- **Filter Application:** All components update simultaneously

---

## 9. Color System

### 9.1 Compliance Status Colors

| Status | Color | Hex | Usage |
|--------|-------|-----|-------|
| Compliant / Under Budget | Green | #22C55E | Target met or exceeded |
| On Target | Blue | #3B82F6 | Within tolerance |
| Warning | Amber | #F59E0B | Approaching threshold |
| Non-Compliant / Over Budget | Red | #EF4444 | Exceeds tolerance |

### 9.2 Variance Colors

| Variance | Color | Meaning |
|----------|-------|---------|
| Favorable (savings) | Green #22C55E | Actual < Budget |
| Neutral | Gray #6B7280 | Within Вұ2% |
| Unfavorable (overrun) | Red #EF4444 | Actual > Budget |

### 9.3 Chart Palette

| Element | Color | Usage |
|---------|-------|-------|
| Actual | #3B82F6 (Blue) | Primary data series |
| Budget/Planned | #94A3B8 (Gray) | Comparison baseline |
| Target Line | #22C55E (Green) | Goal indicator |
| Kinki Sharyo | #8B5CF6 (Purple) | Fleet identifier |
| Alstom | #06B6D4 (Cyan) | Fleet identifier |

---

### 10.1 Breakpoints

| Desktop XL | 1920px+ | Full 4-column KPI row |
| Desktop | 1440px | 4-column KPI, charts side-by-side |
| Laptop | 1280px | 2x2 KPI grid, stacked charts |
| Tablet | 1024px | 2x2 KPI grid, single-column charts |
| Mobile | 640px | Single column, scrollable KPIs |

---

## 11. Interaction Patterns

### 11.1 Period Selection

- **Presets:** Month, Quarter, YTD, Custom
- **Custom Range:** Date picker with validation
- **Quick Navigation:** Previous/Next arrows
- **All Views Sync:** Period change updates all tabs

### 11.2 Drill-Down Navigation

| Click Target | Destination |
|--------------|-------------|
| PM Compliance KPI | Schedule Compliance tab |
| Cost KPI | Cost Compliance tab |
| Alert item | Relevant detail view |
| Chart data point | Detailed breakdown modal |
| Table row | Entity detail page |

### 11.3 Export Options

| Format | Content | Use Case |
|--------|---------|----------|
| PDF | Full report with charts | Executive presentations |
| Excel | Raw data with formulas | Further analysis |
| CSV | Table data only | System integration |
| Image | Individual charts | Reports/emails |

---

## 12. Performance Requirements

### 12.1 Load Times

| Metric | Target | Maximum |
|--------|--------|---------|
| Initial Page Load | <2s | 4s |
| Tab Switch | <500ms | 1s |
| Period Change | <1s | 2s |
| Export Generation | <5s | 15s |

### 12.2 Data Refresh

| Data Type | Refresh Interval |
|-----------|------------------|
| Real-time KPIs | 5 minutes |
| Charts/Trends | On demand |
| Reports | Daily batch |
| Alerts | Push notification |

---

## 13. Unknown Unknowns Discovered

### 13.1 Business Logic

1. **Compliance aggregation:** How to weight sub-metrics into overall score?
2. **Tolerance bands:** Different tolerances for different PM levels?
3. **Cost allocation:** How are shared costs split between fleets?
4. **Baseline adjustments:** When can budget baselines be updated?
5. **Seasonality:** Are there seasonal patterns in mileage/cost compliance?

### 13.2 UX Challenges

6. **Alert fatigue:** How many alerts before users ignore them?
7. **Drill-down depth:** How many levels before users get lost?
8. **Comparison periods:** What comparisons are most valuable (MoM, YoY)?
9. **Goal setting:** Who sets compliance targets and can they be adjusted?
10. **Notification preferences:** Email vs in-app vs SMS for alerts?

### 13.3 Technical Challenges

11. **Historical data retention:** How far back to keep detailed data?
12. **Data reconciliation:** Handling discrepancies between systems?
13. **Offline access:** Do users need offline compliance reports?
14. **Multi-currency:** Are there cross-border cost considerations?
15. **Audit trail:** What compliance changes need to be logged?

### 13.4 Integration

16. **Maximo cost sync:** How are actual costs imported?
17. **Budget import:** Source system for budget data?
18. **External reporting:** Format for regulatory compliance reports?
19. **Dashboard embedding:** Can dashboards be embedded elsewhere?
20. **Single Sign-On:** Integration with enterprise identity?

---

## 14. Sources & References

### 14.1 Maintenance KPIs

- [Fabrico: 7 Essential Maintenance KPIs for 2025](https://www.fabrico.io/blog/maintenance-kpi-dashboard-guide/)
- [MaintainX: Maintenance KPIs Guide](https://www.getmaintainx.com/blog/beginners-guide-maintenance-kpis)
- [Limble CMMS: The Simple Guide to Maintenance KPIs](https://limblecmms.com/learn/metrics/kpi/)
- [eMaint: Guide to Maintenance KPIs](https://www.emaint.com/works/cmms-kpi/)

### 14.2 Variance & Financial Visualization

- [ZebraBI: Variance Analysis in Excel](https://zebrabi.com/variance-analysis-excel/)
- [Microsoft Power BI: Actual vs Budget Charts](https://powerbi.microsoft.com/en-us/blog/actual-vs-budget-which-chart-is-effective/)
- [Vena Solutions: Variance Report Best Practices](https://www.venasolutions.com/blog/4-best-practices-creating-variance-analysis-report-excel)
- [Explo: Compliance Dashboards Best Practices](https://www.explo.co/blog/compliance-dashboards-compliance-management-reporting)

### 14.3 Fleet Maintenance

- [RTA Fleet: Increasing PM Compliance](https://rtafleet.com/blog/fleet-success-story-increasing-vehicle-availability-pm-compliance)
- [Fleetio: Fleet Maintenance Spreadsheet](https://www.fleetio.com/blog/fleet-maintenance-spreadsheet)
- [Fiix: Preventive Maintenance Compliance](https://fiixsoftware.com/maintenance-metrics/preventive-maintenance-compliance/)

---

## 15. Appendix: TypeScript Types Reference

See `src/mileage/types/resource.ts` for existing cost and resource types:
- `CostBaseline` - Per-activity cost baseline
- `CostProjection` - Period cost forecast
- `CostCompliance` - Budget vs actual tracking
- `ManHourAllocation` - Resource utilization
- `ManHourConfig` - Capacity configuration

---

PatternFly Accessibility Features ### WCAG 2.1 AA Built-In | Requirement | PatternFly Support | |------------|-------------------| | **Color Contrast** | All components meet 4.5:1 ratio | | **Keyboard Navigation** | Tab, Arrow, Enter, Escape all supported | | **Screen Reader** | ARIA labels, roles, live regions | | **Focus Indicators** | Visible focus ring on all interactive elements | | **Motion Preferences** | Respects `prefers-reduced-motion` | | **Mobile Touch** | Touch-friendly hit targets (44px minimum) | ### Accessibility in Your Components **Sortable Table Headers:** ```typescript Train ID ``` **Filter Results:** ```typescript
Showing {filteredCount} of {totalCount} trains
``` **Modal Dialogs:** ```typescript

Schedule Maintenance

Select a date and train...

 ``` --- ## 11. PatternFly with Storybook Integration ### Development Setup PatternFly Elements (web components) uses Storybook at `http://localhost:9001` during development. **PatternFly React (for your case):** - Use Storybook 7+ with PatternFly preset - Component documentation auto-generated - Visual regression testing via Playwright **Recommended Storybook Config:** ```typescript // .storybook/main.ts import type { StorybookConfig } from '@storybook/react-vite'; const config: StorybookConfig = { stories: ['../src/**/*.stories.tsx'], addons: ['@storybook/addon-a11y', '@storybook/addon-links'], framework: '@storybook/react-vite', }; export default config; ``` --- ## 12. Colour System Alignment ### PatternFly Base Colours (Light Mode) Your specs align perfectly with PatternFly's semantic colors: | Purpose | Your Hex | PatternFly Equivalent | |---------|----------|----------------------| | **Success/FIT** | #10B981 | `--pf-token-color-status-success` | | **Warning/RESTRICTED** | #F59E0B | `--pf-token-color-status-warning` | | **Error/UNFIT** | #EF4444 | `--pf-token-color-status-danger` | | **Info** | #3B82F6 | `--pf-token-color-status-info` | | **Primary Text** | #111827 | `--pf-token-text-color` | | **Background** | #F9FAFB | `--pf-token-color-surface` | | **Card Background** | #FFFFFF | `--pf-token-color-surface-light` | **Access via CSS Variables:** ```css :root { --pf-token-color-status-success: #22C55E; --pf-token-color-status-warning: #F59E0B; --pf-token-color-status-danger: #EF4444; } ``` --- ## 13. Performance Considerations ### Bundle Size | Library | Size (gzipped) | Impact | |---------|----------------|--------| | @patternfly/react-core | ~200KB | Required for all components | | @patternfly/react-charts | ~150KB | Include if using charts | | @tanstack/react-table | ~30KB | Optional for advanced table logic | | @tanstack/react-virtual | ~10KB | Only if using virtual scrolling | **Total for full implementation: ~390KB gzipped** ### Performance Targets (Your RFQ) | Metric | PatternFly + Your Code | Target | |--------|----------------------|--------| | **First Contentful Paint** | <1.5s | <1.5s вң… | | **Time to Interactive** | <3s | <3s вң… | | **Largest Contentful Paint** | <2.5s | <2.5s вң… | | **Cumulative Layout Shift** | <0.1 | <0.1 вң… | **Optimizations:** - Lazy load charts library (`react-charts`) - Virtual scroll for 128-train table - Code split by route (dashboard vs reports vs planning) - Cache API responses with TanStack Query --- ## 14. Known Issues & Workarounds ### Vite + PatternFly Build Issue **Issue:** Vite build generates incorrect path references in @patternfly/react-core **Workaround:** ```typescript // vite.config.ts export default { build: { rollupOptions: { output: { manualChunks: { 'patternfly': ['@patternfly/react-core'] } } } } } ``` ### Dark Mode Support PatternFly 6 has light theme default. For dark mode: ```typescript // App.tsx import { useEffect } from 'react'; export function App() { useEffect(() => { if (prefersDarkMode) { document.documentElement.classList.add('pf-v6-theme-dark'); } }, []); } ``` --- ## 15. Recommended Implementation Path ### Phase 1: Foundation (2-3 weeks) 1. Set up Vite + React + TypeScript + PatternFly 2. Create base `Page` layout with Masthead + Sidebar 3. Implement 4 KPI card components 4. Build responsive grid system ### Phase 2: Dashboards (3-4 weeks) 1. Centralised dashboard with all KPI cards + charts 2. Fleet status panels + alerts 3. PM schedule timeline (custom component) 4. Resource utilisation chart ### Phase 3: Data Grids (2-3 weeks) 1. Train mileage grid with TanStack Table + PatternFly Table 2. Implement filtering + sorting + pagination 3. Virtual scrolling for "Show All" mode 4. Row actions menu ### Phase 4: Compliance & Reports (2-3 weeks) 1. Compliance dashboard (5 tabs) 2. All chart types (donut, bar, waterfall, etc.) 3. Report generation UI 4. Export functionality ### Phase 5: Polish & Testing (1-2 weeks) 1. Accessibility audit (axe-core) 2. Responsive design testing 3. Visual regression (Playwright) 4. Performance optimization --- ## 16. Unknown Unknowns from PatternFly Research ### Technical Risks 1. **Vite production builds**: Monitor for path issues with @patternfly/react-core 2. **Victory charts performance**: For 144+ data points in man-hour chart, may need aggregation 3. **Virtual scrolling + filters**: Interaction between TanStack Virtual + sorting/filtering ### UX Risks 1. **Chart color-blindness**: PatternFly charts default to colour-only status; add patterns 2. **Table density**: 128 rows Г— 11 columns exceeds typical density; test on 15" screens 3. **Alert fatigue**: No built-in smart grouping for alerts; design custom logic ### Business Logic 1. **Real-time updates**: PatternFly Table doesn't auto-update on WebSocket; implement custom 2. **Multi-user concurrency**: No built-in conflict resolution if 2 users edit same train 3. **Permission-based UI**: PatternFly supports RBAC but requires custom implementation --- ## Sources **PatternFly Official** - [WCAG 2.1 Grid Pattern](https://www.w3.org/WAI/ARIA/apg/patterns/grid/) - [PatternFly Accessibility](https://www.patternfly.org/) --- ## Summary PatternFly is an excellent fit for your Rolling Stock Mileage Management System. All core requirements map to native components: вң… **Dashboards** - Page, Grid, Card, Flex layouts вң… **Charts** - Area, Line, Bar, Donut, Bullet for all RFQ visualizations вң… **Data Tables** - Table/DataView with sorting, filtering, pagination, virtual scroll вң… **Forms & Filters** - Toolbar, Select, SearchInput вң… **Navigation** - Masthead, Sidebar, Tabs, Breadcrumb вң… **Responsive Design** - Mobile-first grid system (sm/md/lg/xl/2xl) вң… **Accessibility** - WCAG 2.1 AA compliance built-in вң… **Alerts** - Alert, AlertGroup with severity variants **Key advantages:** - Enterprise-grade components battle-tested at Red Hat scale - Comprehensive documentation and Storybook examples - Strong accessibility out of box - Compatible with Vite + React 18 + TypeScript - Flexible design system allowing customization **Recommended approach:** - Use PatternFly for all standard components (buttons, cards, grids, alerts) - Supplement with TanStack Table for advanced table logic - Use @patternfly/react-charts for all visualizations - Keep Tailwind CSS as optional utility layer for custom spacing/styling - Follow PatternFly's semantic color tokens for consistency
